---
title: "3-kinship-structure"
author: "Skye Davis"
date: "2026-06-17"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

# Set up

```{r Packages}

# Data manipulation
library(dartR) # v2.9.7
library(dplyr) # v1.1.4

# Kinship
library(SNPRelate) # v1.30.1
library(gdsfmt) # v1.32.0
library(GENESIS) # v2.26.0
library(GGally) # v2.2.1
library(GWASTools) # v1.42.1
library(plotrix) # v3.8-4

# Structure & Stats
library(hierfstat) # v0.5-11
library(diveRsity) # v1.9.90
library(LEA) # v3.8.0
library(geosphere) # v1.5-18

# Plotting
library(ggplot2) # v3.5.1
library(ggpubr) # v0.6.0

# Other attached packages
library(adegenet) # v2.1.10
library(ade4) # v1.7-22

```

```{r Directories}

# Main directory for data
data_dir <- paste0(getwd(),"/data")

# Input data directory for this script
input_data_dir <- paste0(data_dir,"/sex-data")

# Output data directory for this script (where final unrelated vs related genlight objects will be stored)
output_data_dir <- paste0(data_dir,"/kinship-data")
dir.create(output_data_dir, recursive = TRUE)

# Results directories for this script

pcairelate_result_dir <- paste0(getwd(),"/results/3-kinship-structure/3.1-pcair-pcrelate")
dir.create(paste0(pcairelate_result_dir,"/_PLOTS"), recursive = TRUE)

pcoa_result_dir <- paste0(getwd(),"/results/3-kinship-structure/3.2-PCOA")
dir.create(paste0(pcoa_result_dir,"/_PLOTS"), recursive = TRUE)

dapc_result_dir <- paste0(getwd(),"/results/3-kinship-structure/3.3-DAPC")
dir.create(paste0(dapc_result_dir,"/_PLOTS"), recursive = TRUE)

lea_result_dir <- paste0(getwd(),"/results/3-kinship-structure/3.4-LEA")
dir.create(paste0(lea_result_dir,"/_PLOTS"), recursive = TRUE)

struc_result_dir <- paste0(getwd(),"/results/3-kinship-structure/3.5-STRUCTURE")
dir.create(paste0(struc_result_dir,"/_PLOTS"), recursive = TRUE)

stats_result_dir <- paste0(getwd(),"/results/3-kinship-structure/3.6-Stats")
dir.create(paste0(stats_result_dir,"/_PLOTS"), recursive = TRUE)

saa_result_dir <- paste0(getwd(),"/results/3-kinship-structure/3.7-SAA")
dir.create(paste0(saa_result_dir,"/_PLOTS"), recursive = TRUE)

spca_result_dir <- paste0(getwd(),"/results/3-kinship-structure/3.8-sPCA")
dir.create(paste0(spca_result_dir,"/_PLOTS"), recursive = TRUE)

sexbias_result_dir <- paste0(getwd(),"/results/3-kinship-structure/3.9-sex-bias")
dir.create(paste0(sexbias_result_dir,"/_PLOTS"), recursive = TRUE)

kinship_result_dir <- paste0(getwd(),"/results/3-kinship-structure/3.10-kinship")
dir.create(paste0(kinship_result_dir,"/_PLOTS"), recursive = TRUE)

```

# Analysis

## 3.1 PCAiR & PCRelate - Identify close relatives

```{r Create input files}

# Load the filtered SNP data with sex info
setwd(input_data_dir)
data <- readRDS("BTF-3-155-14808-neutral-auto-genlight-sexinfo.RData")

# Create gds
data2 <- gi2gl(gl2gi(data))
gl2gds(data2, outfile="BTF-3-155-14808-neutral-auto-genlight-sexinfo.gds", outpath = input_data_dir)

# For the PC-AiR analysis, build a population dataframe in the format of an AnnotatedDataFrame.
popInfo <- data@other$ind.metrics %>% select(c(id,pop))
colnames(popInfo) <- c("sample.id","Population")
rownames(popInfo) <- NULL
setwd(output_data_dir)
saveRDS(popInfo, file = "PC-AiR-popInfo.RData")

```

```{r Run PC-AiR and PCRelate}

# Load input files
setwd(input_data_dir)
data <- readRDS("BTF-3-155-14808-neutral-auto-genlight-sexinfo.RData")
setwd(input_data_dir)
Snp_gds <- snpgdsOpen("BTF-3-155-14808-neutral-auto-genlight-sexinfo.gds")
setwd(output_data_dir)
popInfo <- readRDS("PC-AiR-popInfo.RData")

# Basic ggplot theme settings
plot_margin <- theme(plot.margin=margin(t = 10, r = 10, b = 5, l = 10, unit = "pt"))
plot_theme <- theme(panel.grid.major = element_blank(), panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), panel.background = element_rect(fill = NA), plot.background = element_rect(fill=NA), panel.border = element_rect(fill=NA),legend.spacing.y = unit(0.01, "cm"),legend.key = element_rect(fill = "transparent")) 
text_size <- theme(axis.title.x = element_text(size = 10), axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8), axis.title.y = element_text(size = 10), axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),plot.title=element_text(size=11,face="bold",hjust=0),legend.text = element_text(size=8), legend.title = element_text(size=8,face="bold"))

# Pop colours for plots
pop_cols <- c("North" = "#ff5f00", "Central" = "#dd217d", "South" = "#725def")

### 1. KING COEFFICIENTS

# Calculate KING kinship coefficients
king <- snpgdsIBDKING(Snp_gds, autosome.only = FALSE)
kingMat <- king$kinship
colnames(kingMat) <- rownames(kingMat) <- king$sample.id
kinship <- snpgdsIBDSelection(king)

# Save plot of kinship vs IBSO
setwd(paste0(pcairelate_result_dir,"/_PLOTS"))
jpeg("PCAiRelate-Step-1-KING-kinship-coeffs-v2.jpg")
ggplot(kinship, aes(IBS0, kinship)) +
    geom_hline(yintercept=2^(-seq(3,9,2)/2), linetype="dashed", color="grey") +
    geom_point(alpha=0.5) +
    ylab("Kinship estimate") +
    theme_bw()
dev.off()


### 2. STANDARD PCA & PLOTS A-B

# Run PCA
std_pca <- snpgdsPCA(Snp_gds, autosome.only = FALSE) # include all chroms (no sex chroms present anyways)

# variance proportion (%) (prop variance explained by each PC)
pc.percent <- std_pca$varprop*100
head(round(pc.percent, 2))

# Make a data.frame of sample id, pop and PC scores
tab <- data.frame(sample.id = std_pca$sample.id,
    pop = factor(popInfo$Population),
    PC1 = std_pca$eigenvect[,1],    # the first eigenvector
    PC2 = std_pca$eigenvect[,2],
    PC3 = std_pca$eigenvect[,3],
    PC4 = std_pca$eigenvect[,4],
    PC5 = std_pca$eigenvect[,5],
    PC6 = std_pca$eigenvect[,6],
    PC7 = std_pca$eigenvect[,7],
    PC8 = std_pca$eigenvect[,8],
    PC9 = std_pca$eigenvect[,9],
    PC10 = std_pca$eigenvect[,10],
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
tab

# Order of pop names
mypoporder <- c("North","Central","South")
tab$pop <- factor(tab$pop, levels = mypoporder)

# Basic Plot (PC1 vs PC2)
A <- ggplot(tab, aes(PC1, PC2, color=pop)) +
    geom_point(size=1) +
    stat_ellipse(geom = "path", aes(group = pop)) +
    labs(x = paste("PC1 (", round(pc.percent[1], digits = 2), "%)", sep = ""), 
       y = paste("PC2 (", round(pc.percent[2], digits = 2), "%)", sep = ""), 
       color = "Region") +  
    scale_color_manual(values=pop_cols)+
    ggtitle(label="Standard PCA")+
    guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(linetype = "blank")))
A

# Parallel Coordinates Plot (All PC's)
B <- ggparcoord(tab, columns=3:12, groupColumn="pop", scale="uniminmax") +
    scale_color_manual(values=pop_cols)+
    ggtitle(label="")+
    scale_x_discrete(labels=c("PC1" = "1", "PC2" = "2","PC3" = "3","PC4" = "4","PC5" = "5","PC6" = "6","PC7" = "7","PC8" = "8","PC9" = "9","PC10" = "10"))+
    labs(x = "Principal component", y = "Individual scores", color = "Region")
B

A2 <- A + plot_margin + plot_theme + text_size
B2 <- B + plot_margin + plot_theme + text_size + theme(legend.position = "none")

# Save plots
setwd(paste0(pcairelate_result_dir,"/_PLOTS"))
tiff("PCAiRelate-Step-2-Standard-PCA.tif",width = 12, height = 12, unit='cm', res=300)
ggarrange(A2,B2, ncol = 1, nrow = 2)
dev.off()



### 3. UNRELATED PCA & PROJECT RELATIVES

# First, we partition the samples into a related and unrelated set. We use a kinship threshold of degree 3 (unrelated is less than first cousins). We load the GENESIS package. In the first iteration, we use the KING estimates for both kinship (kinMat) and ancestry divergence (divMat). KING kinship estimates are negative for samples with different ancestry. 
# Thresholds of 0.025 (~ 2^(-11/2) are recommended for most cases, and should exclude all third and some fourth-degree relatives)

sampset <- pcairPartition(kinobj=kingMat, kin.thresh=0.025,
                          divobj=kingMat, div.thresh=-0.025)

# Unrelated subset
sampset$unrels
# Related subset
sampset$rels

# Perform pca on the unrelated subset
pca.unrel <- snpgdsPCA(Snp_gds, sample.id=sampset$unrels, autosome.only = FALSE)

# Project values for relatives

# snpgdsPCASNPLoadings = Calculate the SNP loadings (or SNP eigenvectors) from the principal component analysis conducted in snpgdsPCA.
snp.load <- snpgdsPCASNPLoading(pca.unrel, gdsobj=Snp_gds)

# snpgdsPCASampLoading = Calculate the sample eigenvectors using the specified SNP loadings â€“ aka Project (related) individuals onto existing principal component axes (based on unrelated samples)
samp.load <- snpgdsPCASampLoading(snp.load, gdsobj=Snp_gds, sample.id=sampset$rels)

# combine unrelated and related PCs and order as in GDS file
pcs <- rbind(pca.unrel$eigenvect, samp.load$eigenvect)
rownames(pcs) <- c(pca.unrel$sample.id, samp.load$sample.id)
samp.ord <- match(std_pca$sample.id, rownames(pcs))
pcs <- pcs[samp.ord,]



### 4. ANCESTRY INFORMATIVE PC'S & PLOT C

# Next we need to determine which PCs are ancestry informative. To do this we need population information. This information is stored in an AnnotatedDataFrame, which is a data.frame with optional metadata describing the colunms (popInfo objected created earlier).

# Make a parallel coordinates plot, color-coding by population.
pc.df <- as.data.frame(pcs)
names(pc.df) <- 1:ncol(pcs)
pc.df$sample.id <- row.names(pcs)
pc.df$pop <- popInfo$Population
pc.df <- pc.df %>% select(sample.id,pop,everything())

# Examine the plot - which PC's are ancestry informative? Which PC's describe the most structure now that close relatives are accounted for?
C <- ggparcoord(pc.df, columns=3:12, groupColumn="pop", scale="uniminmax") +
    scale_color_manual(values=pop_cols)+
    ggtitle(label="Ancestry Informative PC's")+
    scale_x_discrete(labels=c("PC1" = "1", "PC2" = "2","PC3" = "3","PC4" = "4","PC5" = "5","PC6" = "6","PC7" = "7","PC8" = "8","PC9" = "9","PC10" = "10"))+
    labs(x = "Principal component", y = "Individual scores", color = "Region")
C

C2 <- C + plot_margin + plot_theme + text_size + theme(legend.position = "none")

# Save plot
setwd(paste0(pcairelate_result_dir,"/_PLOTS"))
tiff("PCAiRelate-Step-4-Ancestry-informative-PCs.tif",width = 12, height = 12, unit='cm', res=300)
ggarrange(B2,C2, ncol = 1, nrow = 2)
dev.off()



### 5. KINSHIP ACCOUNTING FOR ANCESTRY

# The next step involves a different format of the same gds dataset, so we need to close the current one before opening it again.
snpgdsClose(Snp_gds)

# Create a GenotypeData class object (a SNPGDSFileClass object won't work in PC-Relate, but is required for SNPRelate, hence the change here - its the same data just in a different format stored in R)
setwd(input_data_dir)
Snp_gds <- GdsGenotypeReader("BTF-3-155-14808-neutral-auto-genlight-sexinfo.gds")
Snp_gds <- GenotypeData(Snp_gds)

# Run PC-Relate 

# Set pcs=pcs[,n] where n = the PC's that described the most structure above, e.g. if the first two PC's described structure, use [,1:2] (after combining R and U PC's)
genoData <- GenotypeBlockIterator(Snp_gds)
mypcrelate <- pcrelate(genoData, pcs=pcs[,1:3], 
                       training.set = sampset$unrels)

mypcrelate_data <- mypcrelate$kinBtwn

# Save plot of kinship vs IBSO. The intercepts for relatedness are: 0.35355339 0.17677670 0.08838835 0.04419417
setwd(paste0(pcairelate_result_dir,"/_PLOTS"))
jpeg("PCAiRelate-Step-5-Updated-kinship-coeffs.jpg")
ggplot(mypcrelate_data, aes(k0, kin)) +
    geom_hline(yintercept=2^(-seq(3,9,2)/2), linetype="dashed", color="grey") +
    geom_point(alpha=0.5) +
    xlab("k0")+
    ylab("Kinship estimate") +
    theme_bw()
dev.off()



### 6. ADJUST ANCESTRY USING KINSHIP & PLOTS D-E

# Make a genetic relationship matrix (GRM)
kin_matrix <- pcrelateToMatrix(mypcrelate, scaleKin = 2)

# Run PC-AiR

# Simultaneously partitions unrelated/related and runs the PCA, but uses the updated kinship estimates for the kinMat parameter (rather than the KING estimates used earlier which did not account for ancestry) 
# Note threshold of 2^(-9/2) (~0.044) is equivalent to 3rd degree relatives, use 2^(-11/2) (~0.022) for fourth degree relatives as the cut-off for relatedness. Conomos et al. 2015 recomends 0.025 with dense SNP genotyping data which should identify third-degree (and some fourth-degree) relatives

pca <- pcair(Snp_gds, kinobj=kin_matrix, kin.thresh=0.025, divobj=kingMat, div.thresh=-0.025, unrel.set = sampset$unrels,autosome.only = FALSE)

# The default plot for a 'pcair' object is to plot PC values as black dots and blue pluses for individuals in the â€śunrelated subsetâ€ť and â€śrelated subsetsâ€ť respectively. vx and vy set the PC's to plot.
plot(pca, vx=1, vy=2)
plot(pca, vx=2, vy=3)

# Extract % variance between pops explained by PC's
pc.percent <- pca$varprop*100
head(round(pc.percent, 2))

# Prepare results for plotting
# pcs_IT1 = iteration 1 (so it doesnt replace the pcs object created in Step 8)
pcs_IT1 <- pca$vectors
pc.df_IT1 <- as.data.frame(pcs_IT1)
names(pc.df_IT1) <- paste0("PC", 1:ncol(pcs_IT1))
pc.df_IT1$sample.id <- row.names(pcs_IT1)
pc.df_IT1$pop <- popInfo$Population
pc.df_IT1 <- pc.df_IT1 %>% select(sample.id,pop,everything())
pc.df_IT1

# Order of pop names
mypoporder <- c("North","Central","South")
pc.df_IT1$pop <- factor(pc.df_IT1$pop, levels = mypoporder)

# Basic Plot (PC1 vs PC2)
D <- ggplot(pc.df_IT1, aes(PC1, PC2, color=pop)) +
    geom_point(size=1) +
    stat_ellipse(geom = "path", aes(group = pop)) +
    labs(x = paste("PC1 (", round(pc.percent[1], digits = 2), "%)", sep = ""), 
       y = paste("PC2 (", round(pc.percent[2], digits = 2), "%)", sep = ""), 
       color = "Region") +  
    scale_color_manual(values=pop_cols)+
    guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(linetype = "blank")))
D

E <- ggplot(pc.df_IT1, aes(PC2, PC3, color=pop)) +
    geom_point(size=1) +
    stat_ellipse(geom = "path", aes(group = pop)) +
    labs(x = paste("PC2 (", round(pc.percent[2], digits = 2), "%)", sep = ""), 
       y = paste("PC3 (", round(pc.percent[3], digits = 2), "%)", sep = ""), 
       color = "Region") +  
    scale_color_manual(values=pop_cols)+
    guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(linetype = "blank")))
E

D2 <- D + plot_margin + plot_theme + text_size
E2 <- E + plot_margin + plot_theme + text_size + theme(legend.position = "none")

# Save plot
setwd(paste0(pcairelate_result_dir,"/_PLOTS"))
tiff("PCAiRelate-Step-6-Adjusted-PCA-Iteration1.tif",width = 10, height = 12, unit='cm', res=300)
ggarrange(D2,E2, ncol = 1, nrow = 2)
dev.off()



### 7. REPEAT STEPS 5-6 & PLOTS F-G

# Step 5 computed kinship accounting for ancestry and step 6 performed a PCA accounting for the kinship computed by Step 5. Generally a second iteration of these steps is required to ensure convergence. This final stage will comprise of:
### Step 7a - compute kinship using updated ancestry from step 6
### Step 7b - compute ancestry using updated kinship from step 11
# We can check for convergence by correlating the top 5 PC's from each step of PC-AiR with eachother

# Step 7a - update kinship using Step 6 PC's (pcs_IT1)
mypcrelate_IT2 <- pcrelate(genoData, pcs=pcs_IT1[,1:2], 
                       training.set = sampset$unrels)
mypcrelate_IT2_data <- mypcrelate_IT2$kinBtwn

# Step 7b - updated ancestry using Step 11's kinship
kin_matrix_IT2 <- pcrelateToMatrix(mypcrelate_IT2, scaleKin = 2)

pca_IT2 <- pcair(Snp_gds, kinobj=kin_matrix_IT2, kin.thresh=0.025, divobj=kingMat, div.thresh=-0.025, unrel.set = sampset$unrels,autosome.only = FALSE)

plot(pca_IT2, vx=1, vy=2)
plot(pca_IT2, vx=2, vy=3)

# Variance explained
pc.percent <- pca_IT2$varprop*100
head(round(pc.percent, 2))

pcs_IT2 <- pca$vectors
pc.df_IT2 <- as.data.frame(pcs_IT2)
names(pc.df_IT2) <- paste0("PC", 1:ncol(pcs_IT2))
pc.df_IT2$sample.id <- row.names(pcs_IT2)
pc.df_IT2$pop <- popInfo$Population
pc.df_IT2 <- pc.df_IT2 %>% select(sample.id,pop,everything())

# Order of pop names
mypoporder <- c("North","Central","South")
pc.df_IT2$pop <- factor(pc.df_IT2$pop, levels = mypoporder)

# Basic Plot (PC1 vs PC2)
F1 <- ggplot(pc.df_IT2, aes(PC1, PC2, color=pop)) +
    geom_point(size=1) +
    stat_ellipse(geom = "path", aes(group = pop)) +
    labs(x = paste("PC1 (", round(pc.percent[1], digits = 2), "%)", sep = ""), 
       y = paste("PC2 (", round(pc.percent[2], digits = 2), "%)", sep = ""), 
       color = "Region") +  
    scale_color_manual(values=pop_cols)+
    guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(linetype = "blank")))
F1

G <- ggplot(pc.df_IT2, aes(PC2, PC3, color=pop)) +
    geom_point(size=1) +
    stat_ellipse(geom = "path", aes(group = pop)) +
    labs(x = paste("PC2 (", round(pc.percent[2], digits = 2), "%)", sep = ""), 
       y = paste("PC3 (", round(pc.percent[3], digits = 2), "%)", sep = ""), 
       color = "Region") +  
    scale_color_manual(values=pop_cols)+
    guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(linetype = "blank")))
G

F2 <- F1 + plot_margin + plot_theme + text_size
G2 <- G + plot_margin + plot_theme + text_size + theme(legend.position = "none")

# Save plot
setwd(paste0(pcairelate_result_dir,"/_PLOTS"))
tiff("PCAiRelate-Step-7-Adjusted-PCA-Iteration2.tif",width = 10, height = 12, unit='cm', res=300)
ggarrange(F2,G2, ncol = 1, nrow = 2)
dev.off()



### 8. COMPARISON OF ITERATIONS & PLOTS H-K

# Typically 2 iterations of the process is sufficient (as performed above). It's important to visually and mathematically confirm convergence before settling on the results of the second iteration

# Check correlation between PC's for iteration 1 and 2
cor(pc.df_IT1$PC1, pc.df_IT2$PC1, method = c("pearson")) # corr = 1
cor(pc.df_IT1$PC2, pc.df_IT2$PC2, method = c("pearson")) # corr = 1
cor(pc.df_IT1$PC3, pc.df_IT2$PC3, method = c("pearson")) # corr = 1

# Multi-plot comparing Iteration 1 and 2 - visually check convergence

# Plot for Iteration 1 Kinship (object mypcrelate_data)
H <- ggplot(mypcrelate_data, aes(k0, kin))+
    geom_point(alpha=0.5) +
    xlab("k0")+
    ylab("Kinship estimate") +
    ggtitle(label="Iteration 1 Kinship")+
    theme_bw()
H

# Plot for Iteration 2 Kinship (object mypcrelate_IT2_data)
I <- ggplot(mypcrelate_IT2_data, aes(k0, kin)) +
    geom_point(alpha=0.5) +
    xlab("k0")+
    ylab("Kinship estimate") +
    ggtitle(label="Iteration 2 Kinship")+
    theme_bw()
I

# Plot for Iteration 1 PCA (object pc.df_IT1)
J <- ggplot(pc.df_IT1, aes(PC1, PC2, color=pop)) +
    geom_point(size=1) +
    scale_color_manual(values=pop_cols)+
    ggtitle(label="Iteration 1 PCA")+
    theme_classic()
J

# Plot for Iteration 2 PCA (object pc.df_IT2)
K <- ggplot(pc.df_IT2, aes(PC1, PC2, color=pop)) +
    geom_point(size=1) +
    scale_color_manual(values=pop_cols)+
    ggtitle(label="Iteration 2 PCA")+
    theme_classic()
K

# Save plots
setwd(paste0(pcairelate_result_dir,"/_PLOTS"))
tiff("PCAiRelate-Step-8-Iteration1v2.tif",width = 17, height = 12, unit='cm', res=300)
ggarrange(H,I,J,K, ncol = 2, nrow = 2)
dev.off()



### 9. SUMMARY PLOTS

# This produces a multi plot that shows standard and PC-AiR PCA's side-by-side - both PC1*PC2 plot and the parallel coordinates plots. Use Iteration 2 results for the PC-AiR PCA's.

# Standard PCA - PC1*PC2
pc.percent <- std_pca$varprop*100
head(round(pc.percent, 2))
A <- ggplot(tab, aes(PC1, PC2, color=pop)) +
    geom_point(size=1) +
    stat_ellipse(geom = "path", aes(group = pop)) +
    labs(x = paste("PC1 (", round(pc.percent[1], digits = 2), "%)", sep = ""), 
       y = paste("PC2 (", round(pc.percent[2], digits = 2), "%)", sep = ""), 
       color = "Region") +  
    scale_color_manual(values=pop_cols)+
    ggtitle(label="Standard PCA")+
    guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(linetype = "blank")))
A


# Standard PCA - Parallel Coordinates Plot (All PC's)
B <- ggparcoord(tab, columns=3:12, groupColumn="pop", scale="uniminmax") +
    scale_color_manual(values=pop_cols)+
    ggtitle(label="")+
    scale_x_discrete(labels=c("PC1" = "1", "PC2" = "2","PC3" = "3","PC4" = "4","PC5" = "5","PC6" = "6","PC7" = "7","PC8" = "8","PC9" = "9","PC10" = "10"))+
    labs(x = "Principal component", y = "Individual scores", color = "Region")
B

# PC-AiR PCA - PC1*PC2 (USE ITERATION 2)
pc.percent <- pca_IT2$varprop*100
head(round(pc.percent, 2))
K <- ggplot(pc.df_IT2, aes(PC1, PC2, color=pop)) +
    geom_point(size=1) +
    stat_ellipse(geom = "path", aes(group = pop)) +
    labs(x = paste("PC1 (", round(pc.percent[1], digits = 2), "%)", sep = ""), 
       y = paste("PC2 (", round(pc.percent[2], digits = 2), "%)", sep = ""), 
       color = "Region") + 
    scale_color_manual(values=pop_cols)+
    ggtitle(label="PC-AiR PCA")+
    guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(linetype = "blank")))
K


# PC-AiR PCA - Parallel Coordinates Plot (All PC's)
L <- ggparcoord(pc.df_IT2, columns=3:12, groupColumn="pop", scale="uniminmax") +
    scale_color_manual(values=pop_cols)+
    ggtitle(label="")+
    scale_x_discrete(labels=c("PC1" = "1", "PC2" = "2","PC3" = "3","PC4" = "4","PC5" = "5","PC6" = "6","PC7" = "7","PC8" = "8","PC9" = "9","PC10" = "10"))+
    labs(x = "Principal component", y = "Individual scores", color = "Region")
L

# Save plots
plot_margin <- theme(plot.margin=margin(t = 10, r = 10, b = 5, l = 10, unit = "pt"))
plot_theme <- theme(panel.grid.major = element_blank(), panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), panel.background = element_rect(fill = NA), plot.background = element_rect(fill=NA), panel.border = element_rect(fill=NA),legend.spacing.y = unit(0.01, "cm"),legend.key = element_rect(fill = "transparent")) 
text_size <- theme(axis.title.x = element_text(size = 10), axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8), axis.title.y = element_text(size = 10), axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),plot.title=element_text(size=11,face="bold",hjust=0),legend.text = element_text(size=8), legend.title = element_text(size=8,face="bold"))

A2 <- A + plot_margin + plot_theme + text_size + theme(legend.position = "none")
B2 <- B + plot_margin + plot_theme + text_size
K2 <- K + plot_margin + plot_theme + text_size + theme(legend.position = "none")
L2 <- L + plot_margin + plot_theme + text_size

# Save plot - PCAs and coordinate plots

setwd(paste0(pcairelate_result_dir,"/_PLOTS"))
tiff("PCAiRelate-Step-9-summary-plots1.tif",width = 17.5, height = 12, unit='cm', res=300)
ggarrange(A2,B2,K2,L2, ncol = 2, nrow = 2)
dev.off()

# Save plot - PCAs only

A2 <- A + plot_margin + plot_theme + text_size
K2 <- K + plot_margin + plot_theme + text_size

setwd(paste0(pcairelate_result_dir,"/_PLOTS"))
tiff("PCAiRelate-Step-9-summary-plots2.tif",width = 12, height = 12, unit='cm', res=300)
ggarrange(A2,K2,ncol = 1, nrow = 2)
dev.off()


### 10. SAVE RESULTS

# Save kinship data from iteration 2
setwd(pcairelate_result_dir)
saveRDS(mypcrelate_IT2_data, file = "mypcrelate_IT2_data.RData")

# Save as csv
setwd(pcairelate_result_dir)
write.csv(mypcrelate_IT2_data,file = "PCRelate-updated-kinship-dataframe-iteration2.csv",row.names = FALSE)

# Save kinship matrix from iteration 2
setwd(pcairelate_result_dir)
saveRDS(kin_matrix_IT2, file = "kin_matrix_IT2.RData")


```

```{r Separate related and unrelated datasets}

# Load filtered SNP data with sex info
setwd(input_data_dir)
data <- readRDS("BTF-3-155-14808-neutral-auto-genlight-sexinfo.RData")

# Load iteration 2 kinship matrix
setwd(pcairelate_result_dir)
kin_matrix_IT2 <- readRDS("kin_matrix_IT2.RData")

# Identify close relatives again - check the kinship plots from step 5 to see the cut-off for close relatives, since kinship coeff thresholds are diff from KING
sampset <- pcairPartition(kinobj=kin_matrix_IT2, kin.thresh=0.17677670,
                          divobj=kin_matrix_IT2, div.thresh=-0.17677670)

# How many unrelated?
length(sampset$unrels)
# How many related?
length(sampset$rels)

# Save datasets

# Unrelated only
data2 <- gl.drop.ind(data,ind.list = sampset$rels, recalc = TRUE,mono.rm = TRUE)
setwd(output_data_dir)
saveRDS(data2, file = "BTF-3-107-14788-neutral-auto-sex-unrel.RData")

# Related only
data3 <- gl.keep.ind(data,ind.list = sampset$rels, recalc = TRUE,mono.rm = TRUE)
setwd(output_data_dir)
saveRDS(data3, file = "BTF-3-48-13993-neutral-auto-sex-relonly.RData")

# Save final sample metadata
setwd(output_data_dir)
write.csv(data2@other$ind.metrics,"BTF-final-samples-metadata-unrelated.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(data3@other$ind.metrics,"BTF-final-samples-metadata-related.csv", row.names = FALSE)

```

NOTE: All downstream analyses use the 'unrelated' dataset unless otherwise specified

## 3.2 Principal coordinates analysis (PCoA)

```{r PCoA}

# Load unrelated data
setwd(output_data_dir)
data <- readRDS("BTF-3-107-14788-neutral-auto-sex-unrel.RData")

# Convert to gi and scale
data <- gl2gi(data)
data_scaled <- scaleGen(data, center = FALSE, scale = TRUE, NA.method = "mean")

# Perform PCoA
pca1 <- dudi.pco(dist(data_scaled), scannf = FALSE,nf = 3)
pca1_df <- cbind(as.data.frame(pca1$li), data$pop)
colnames(pca1_df) <- c("Axis1","Axis2","Axis3","pop")

# Order of pop names
mypoporder <- c("North","Central","South")
pca1_df$pop <- factor(pca1_df$pop, levels = mypoporder)

# Save pca dataframe
setwd(pcoa_result_dir)
write.csv(pca1_df,file = "pcoa-scores-by-region-scaled-unrelated.csv")

# Plots

# Pop colours for plots
pop_cols <- c("North" = "#ff5f00", "Central" = "#dd217d", "South" = "#725def")

A <- ggplot(data = pca1_df, aes(x = Axis1, y = Axis2, color = pop))+
  stat_ellipse(geom = "path", aes(group = pop)) +
  geom_point(size = 1)+
  labs(x = paste("PC1 (",round((pca1$eig[1]/sum(pca1$eig))*100,digits=2),"%)", sep=""), y = paste("PC2 (",round((pca1$eig[2]/sum(pca1$eig))*100,digits=2),"%)", sep=""), color = "Region", title = "PC1 vs PC2")+
  guides(fill = guide_legend(byrow = TRUE))+
  scale_color_manual(values = pop_cols) +
  guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(linetype = "blank")))
A

B <- ggplot(data = pca1_df, aes(x = Axis2, y = Axis3, color = pop))+
  stat_ellipse(geom = "path", aes(group = pop)) +
  geom_point(size = 1)+
  labs(x = paste("PC2 (",round((pca1$eig[2]/sum(pca1$eig))*100,digits=2),"%)", sep=""), y = paste("PC3 (",round((pca1$eig[3]/sum(pca1$eig))*100,digits=2),"%)", sep=""), color = "Region", title = "PC2 vs PC3")+
  guides(fill = guide_legend(byrow = TRUE))+
  scale_color_manual(values = pop_cols) +
  guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(linetype = "blank")))
B

# Save plot for publication

plot_margin <- theme(plot.margin=margin(t = 10, r = 10, b = 5, l = 10, unit = "pt"))
plot_theme <- theme(panel.grid.major = element_blank(), panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), panel.background = element_rect(fill = NA), plot.background = element_rect(fill=NA), panel.border = element_rect(fill=NA),legend.spacing.y = unit(0.01, "cm"),legend.key = element_rect(fill = "transparent")) 
text_size <- theme(axis.title.x = element_text(size = 10), axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8), axis.title.y = element_text(size = 10), axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),plot.title=element_text(size=11,face="bold",hjust=0),legend.text = element_text(size=8), legend.title = element_text(size=8,face="bold"))
no_legend <- theme(legend.position="none")

A2 <- A + plot_margin + plot_theme + text_size
B2 <- B + plot_margin + plot_theme + text_size

## TIFF and sized for Science Journal
setwd(paste0(pcoa_result_dir,"/_PLOTS"))
tiff("neutral-PCoA-with-ellipses-regions-scaled-unrelated-1v2.tif",width = 14, height = 10, unit='cm', res=300)
ggarrange(A2,
          labels = c(""),
          ncol = 1, nrow = 1)
dev.off()

setwd(paste0(pcoa_result_dir,"/_PLOTS"))
tiff("neutral-PCoA-with-ellipses-regions-scaled-unrelated-2v3.tif",width = 14, height = 10, unit='cm', res=300)
ggarrange(B2,
          labels = c(""),
          ncol = 1, nrow = 1)
dev.off()

A2 <- A + plot_margin + plot_theme + text_size + no_legend
B2 <- B + plot_margin + plot_theme + text_size + no_legend

setwd(paste0(pcoa_result_dir,"/_PLOTS"))
tiff("neutral-PCoA-with-ellipses-regions-scaled-unrelated-1v2v3.tif",width = 14, height = 6, unit='cm', res=300)
ggarrange(A2,B2,
          labels = c("A","B"),
          ncol = 2, nrow = 1)
dev.off()


```

## 3.3 Discriminant analysis of principal components (DAPC)

```{r DAPC - a priori}

# Load unrelated data
setwd(output_data_dir)
data <- readRDS("BTF-3-107-14788-neutral-auto-sex-unrel.RData")
data <- gl2gi(data)

# Perform DAPC with pop priors
dapc0 <- dapc(data, pop = pop(data), n.pca = 30, n.da = nPop(data)-1, scale = TRUE)

# Choose how many PC's to retain using optim.a.score
opt <- optim.a.score(dapc0, n.pca = 1:30, n.da = nPop(data)-1, smart = FALSE, plot = TRUE, n.sim = 1000)

setwd(dapc_result_dir)
sink(file = paste0("DAPC-unrel-apriori-optim-a-score.txt"))
cat("dapc optim.a.score\n")
capture.output(opt)
sink()

# Perform DAPC with pop priors and opt$best
dapc1 <- dapc(data, pop = pop(data), n.pca = opt$best, n.da = nPop(data)-1, scale = TRUE)

# Quick check
scatter(dapc1)
compoplot(dapc1)

setwd(dapc_result_dir)
sink(file = paste0("DAPC-unrel-apriori-results.txt"))
cat("***dapc summary***\n")
cat("\n")
capture.output(dapc1)
cat("\n")
cat("***Group Membership***\n")
capture.output(summary.dapc(dapc1))
cat("\n")
cat("***Group Membership Probabilities***\n")
capture.output(predict.dapc(dapc1))
sink()

# Get prior vs posterior group assignments
table(dapc1$grp,dapc1$assign)

# Get a dataframe of membership probabilities and group assignments
dapc1_ass <- as.data.frame(memprob$posterior)
dapc1_ass$prior_assign <- dapc1$grp
dapc1_ass$post_assign <- dapc1$assign

# Save membership probabilities and assignments
setwd(dapc_result_dir)
write.csv(dapc1_ass,file = paste0("DAPC-unrel-apriori-memprob.csv"))

setwd(dapc_result_dir)
sink(file = paste0("DAPC-unrel-apriori-prior-post-grps.txt"))
cat(print(table(dapc1$grp,dapc1$assign)))
sink()

# Set K
K <- 3

# Set plot colours
pop_cols <- c("#ff5f00", "#dd217d", "#725def")

# Membership probability plot (STRUCTURE-like plot)
setwd(paste0(dapc_result_dir,"/_PLOTS"))
jpeg("DAPC-unrel-apriori-structureplot.png", width = 600, height = 400)
compoplot(dapc1, posi="bottomright", txt.leg=paste("Cluster", 1:K), lab="", ncol=1, xlab="individuals", col=pop_cols)
dev.off()

# Assignment plot
setwd(paste0(dapc_result_dir,"/_PLOTS"))
jpeg("DAPC-unrel-apriori-assignment-plot.png", width = 600, height = 600)
assignplot(dapc1)
dev.off()

# Extract data for plotting (first discriminant function = LD1)
ind_coords <- as.data.frame(dapc1$ind.coord)
ind_coords$Group <- dapc1$grp

# Eigenvalues
setwd(dapc_result_dir)
sink(file = "DAPC-unrel-apriori-eigenvalues.txt")
capture.output(dapc1$eig)
sink()

# Save data for plotting later
setwd(dapc_result_dir)
saveRDS(dapc1,file = "DAPC-unrel-apriori-dapc1-object.RData")
write.csv(dapc1$tab, file = "DAPC-unrel-apriori-PC-Axes.csv")
write.csv(ind_coords, file = "DAPC-unrel-apriori-LD-Axes.csv")
write.csv(dapc1$var.contr, file = "DAPC-unrel-apriori-var-contributions.csv")

# Save a publication quality plot

# Convert 'pop' to factor with custom order
mypoporder <- c("North","Central","South")
ind_coords$Group <- factor(ind_coords$Group, levels = mypoporder)

# Plot density of LD1 (first discriminant function by group in ggplot)
A <- ggplot(data=ind_coords, aes(x=LD1, group=Group, fill=Group)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.75)+
  scale_fill_manual(values=pop_cols,labels=c("North","Central","South"))+
  labs(x = "LD1", y = "Density")+
  xlim(-10,12)+
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::number_format(accuracy = 0.01))+ # set y-axis to 2 decimal places
  theme_classic()
A

# Plot scatterplot of LD1 and LD2
B <- ggplot(data=ind_coords, aes(x=LD1, y=LD2, color=Group)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.75)+
  stat_ellipse(geom = "path", aes(group = Group)) +
  scale_color_manual(values=pop_cols,labels=c("North","Central","South"))+
  labs(x = "LD1", y = "LD2", color = "Group")
B


# Save plot for publication

plot_margin <- theme(plot.margin=margin(t = 10, r = 10, b = 5, l = 10, unit = "pt"))
plot_theme <- theme(panel.grid.major = element_blank(), panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), panel.background = element_rect(fill = NA), plot.background = element_rect(fill=NA), panel.border = element_rect(fill=NA),legend.spacing.y = unit(0.01, "cm"),legend.key = element_rect(fill = "transparent")) 
text_size <- theme(axis.title.x = element_text(size = 10), axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8), axis.title.y = element_text(size = 10), axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),plot.title=element_text(size=11,face="bold",hjust=0),legend.text = element_text(size=8), legend.title = element_text(size=8,face="bold"))
no_legend <- theme(legend.position="none")

A2 <- A + plot_margin + plot_theme + text_size + no_legend
B2 <- B + plot_margin + plot_theme + text_size + no_legend

setwd(paste0(dapc_result_dir,"/_PLOTS"))
tiff("DAPC-unrel-apriori-LD1-plot.tif",width = 6.5, height = 5, unit='cm', res=300)
ggarrange(A2,
          labels = c(""),
          ncol = 1, nrow = 1)
dev.off()

setwd(paste0(dapc_result_dir,"/_PLOTS"))
tiff("DAPC-unrel-apriori-LD1-LD2-plot.tif",width = 6.5, height = 5, unit='cm', res=300)
ggarrange(B2,
          labels = c(""),
          ncol = 1, nrow = 1)
dev.off()


```

```{r DAPC - de novo}

# Load unrelated data
setwd(output_data_dir)
data <- readRDS("BTF-3-107-14788-neutral-auto-sex-unrel.RData")
data <- gl2gi(data)

# Infer K without pop priors
clusters <- find.clusters(data, max.n.clust = 10, n.pca = 30)

# Preliminary DAPC
dapc0 <- dapc(data, grp = clusters$grp, n.pca = 30, n.da = length(levels(clusters$grp))-1, scale = TRUE)

# Choose how many PC's to retain using optim.a.score
opt <- optim.a.score(dapc0, n.pca = 1:30, n.da = nPop(data)-1, smart = FALSE, plot = TRUE, n.sim = 1000)

setwd(dapc_result_dir)
sink(file = paste0("DAPC-unrel-denovo-optim-a-score.txt"))
cat("dapc optim.a.score\n")
capture.output(opt)
sink()

# Perform DAPC with pop priors and opt$best
dapc1 <- dapc(data, pop = clusters$grp, n.pca = opt$best, n.da = length(levels(clusters$grp))-1, scale = TRUE)

setwd(dapc_result_dir)
sink(file = paste0("DAPC-unrel-denovo-results.txt"))
cat("***dapc summary***\n")
cat("\n")
capture.output(dapc1)
cat("\n")
cat("***Group Membership***\n")
capture.output(summary.dapc(dapc1))
cat("\n")
cat("***Group Membership Probabilities***\n")
capture.output(predict.dapc(dapc1))
sink()

# Examine grp membership probability
memprob <- predict.dapc(dapc1)
memprob$posterior
plot(memprob$posterior[,1],memprob$posterior[,2],xlab="Cluster 1", ylab="Cluster 2") # add more for additional clusters

# Get prior vs posterior group assignments
table(dapc1$grp,dapc1$assign)

# Get a dataframe of membership probabilities and group assignments
dapc1_ass <- as.data.frame(memprob$posterior)
colnames(dapc1_ass) <- c("Cluster1","Cluster2")
dapc1_ass$prior_assign <- dapc1$grp
dapc1_ass$post_assign <- dapc1$assign

# Save membership probabilities and assignments
setwd(dapc_result_dir)
write.csv(dapc1_ass,file = paste0("DAPC-unrel-denovo-memprob.csv"))

setwd(dapc_result_dir)
sink(file = paste0("DAPC-unrel-denovo-prior-post-grps.txt"))
cat(print(table(dapc1$grp,dapc1$assign)))
sink()

# Plotting DAPC results

K <- 2

# Membership probability plot (STRUCTURE-like plot)
setwd(paste0(dapc_result_dir,"/_PLOTS"))
jpeg(paste0("DAPC-unrel-denovo-structureplot.png"), width = 600, height = 400)
compoplot(dapc1, posi="bottomright", txt.leg=paste("Cluster", 1:K), lab="", ncol=1, xlab="individuals", col=funky(K))
dev.off()

# Assignment plot
setwd(paste0(dapc_result_dir,"/_PLOTS"))
jpeg(paste0("DAPC-unrel-denovo-assignment-plot.png"), width = 600, height = 600)
assignplot(dapc1) # Use subset to examine individuals more closely
dev.off()

# Extract data for plotting (first discriminant function = LD1)
ind_coords <- as.data.frame(dapc1$ind.coord)
ind_coords$Group <- dapc1$grp

# Eigenvalues
setwd(dapc_result_dir)
sink(file = paste0("DAPC-unrel-apriori-eigenvalues.txt"))
capture.output(dapc1$eig)
sink()

# Save data for plotting later
setwd(dapc_result_dir)
saveRDS(dapc1,file = paste0("DAPC-unrel-denovo-dapc1-object.RData"))
write.csv(dapc1$tab, file = paste0("DAPC-unrel-denovo-PC-Axes.csv"))
write.csv(ind_coords, file = paste0("DAPC-unrel-denovo-LD-Axes.csv"))
write.csv(dapc1$var.contr, file = paste0("DAPC-unrel-denovo-var-contributions.csv"))

# Save a publication quality plot
setwd(dapc_result_dir)
ind_coords <- read.csv(paste0("DAPC-unrel-denovo-LD-Axes.csv"), header = TRUE)

# Set Group as factor
ind_coords$Group <- as.factor(ind_coords$Group)

# Plot density of LD1 (first discriminant function by group in ggplot)
A <- ggplot(data=ind_coords, aes(x=LD1, group=Group, fill=Group)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.75)+
  scale_fill_manual(values=funky(K), labels = c("Cluster 1", "Cluster 2"))+
  labs(x = "LD1", y = "Density")+
  xlim(-4,6)+ 
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::number_format(accuracy = 0.01))+
  theme_classic()
A

# Save plot for publication
plot_margin <- theme(plot.margin=margin(t = 10, r = 10, b = 5, l = 10, unit = "pt"))
plot_theme <- theme(panel.grid.major = element_blank(), panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), panel.background = element_rect(fill = NA), plot.background = element_rect(fill=NA), panel.border = element_rect(fill=NA),legend.spacing.y = unit(0.01, "cm"),legend.key = element_rect(fill = "transparent")) 
text_size <- theme(axis.title.x = element_text(size = 10), axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8), axis.title.y = element_text(size = 10), axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),plot.title=element_text(size=11,face="bold",hjust=0),legend.text = element_text(size=8), legend.title = element_text(size=8,face="bold"))
no_legend <- theme(legend.position="none")

A2 <- A + plot_margin + plot_theme + text_size + no_legend

setwd(paste0(dapc_result_dir,"/_PLOTS"))
tiff(paste0("DAPC-unrel-denovo-LD1-plot.tif"),width = 6.5, height = 5, unit='cm', res=300)
ggarrange(A2,
          labels = c(""),
          ncol = 1, nrow = 1)
dev.off()


```

## 3.4 LEA

```{r LEA}

# Load unrelated data
setwd(output_data_dir)
data <- readRDS("BTF-3-107-14788-neutral-auto-sex-unrel.RData")

# Convert to geno
gl2geno(data, outfile = "BTF-unrel-LEA-input", outpath=lea_result_dir)

# New project
setwd(lea_result_dir)
snmf_project = LEA::snmf("./BTF-unrel-LEA-input.geno",
                             K = 1:10,entropy = TRUE,
                             repetitions = 100,
                             project = "new",ploidy = 2)

# Choose best K from plot. This is the lowest cross-entropy score. If all equal and flattened out take the first at the level.
setwd(paste0(lea_result_dir,"/_PLOTS"))
jpeg("LEA_entropy_plot_unrelated.jpg")
plot(snmf_project, col = "orange", pch = 19, cex = 1.2)
dev.off()

K = 2

# Save the best run for that K to plot
best = which.min(LEA::cross.entropy(snmf_project, K = K))

# Set an assortment of colours (that matches the number of k you have)
my.colors <- c("firebrick","darkcyan")

setwd(paste0(lea_result_dir,"/_PLOTS"))
jpeg("LEA_ancestry_proportions_unrelated.jpg",width = 600, height = 300)
LEA::barchart(snmf_project, K = K, run = best,
        border = NA, space = 0,
        col = my.colors,
        xlab = "Individuals",
        ylab = "Ancestry proportions",
        main = "Ancestry matrix", sort.by.Q = FALSE) -> bp_K
axis(1, at = 1:length(bp_K$order),
    labels = bp_K$order, las=1,
    cex.axis = .4)
dev.off()

```

## 3.5 STRUCTURE

```{r STRUCTURE - Run}

### SET UP

# Download the non-GUI version of STRUCTURE here: https://web.stanford.edu/group/pritchardlab/structure_software/release_versions/v2.3.4/html/structure.html
# Unzip and specify the location of this directory here:
structure_exec <- "path/to/structure.exe"

### ANALYSIS - INFER K

# Load unrelated data
setwd(output_data_dir)
data_unrel <- readRDS("BTF-3-107-14788-neutral-auto-sex-unrel.RData")

# To save computational time, subsample loci
data_unrel2 <- gl.subsample.loci(data_unrel, method = "random", mono.rm = TRUE, n = 5000)

# Check that these loci still capture the same structure in the data
data3 <- gl2gi(data_unrel2)
data3_scale <- scaleGen(data3,center = FALSE, scale = TRUE, NA.method = "mean")
pca1 <- dudi.pco(dist(data3_scale), scannf = FALSE,nf = 3)
pca1_df <- cbind(as.data.frame(pca1$li), data3$pop) # Colour points by location
colnames(pca1_df) <- c("Axis1","Axis2","Axis3","pop")
A <- ggplot(data = pca1_df, aes(x = Axis1, y = Axis2, color = pop))+
  stat_ellipse(geom = "path", aes(group = pop)) +
  geom_point(size = 1)+
  labs(x = paste("PC1 (",round((pca1$eig[1]/sum(pca1$eig))*100,digits=2),"%)", sep=""), y = paste("PC2 (",round((pca1$eig[2]/sum(pca1$eig))*100,digits=2),"%)", sep=""), color = "Region", title = "PC1 vs PC2")+
  guides(fill = guide_legend(byrow = TRUE))+
  guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(linetype = "blank")))
A

# Save subset
setwd(output_data_dir)
saveRDS(data_unrel2, "BTF-3-107-5000-neutral-auto-sex-unrel.RData")

# Specify what data and run is being conducted for renaming the STRUCTURE output later
data_name <- "BTF-3-107-5000-unrel"
run_name <- "STRUCTURE-inferk-run1" # Multiple runs performed, changing run # each time

# Run STRUCTURE. Some results are stored in the structure_result object but full results are saved in the directory below (only if you set delete.files = FALSE)
setwd(struc_result_dir)
structure_result <- gl.run.structure(x = data_unrel2, exec = structure_exec, plot.out = TRUE, delete.files = FALSE, k.range = c(1:6), num.k.rep = 1, burnin = 10000, numreps = 100000, noadmix = FALSE, freqscorr = TRUE, pop.prior = NULL, inferalpha = TRUE, alpha = 1)

# STRUCTURE output saves with a very long name, using the run and time stamps. Files renamed using code below.

# Define the prefix to remove
prefix_to_remove <- "gtypes.created.on.2024-06-22.22.46.14.structureRun" # changed as needed

# Rename the root folder where results were saved
setwd(struc_result_dir)
file.rename(prefix_to_remove, paste(data_name,run_name,sep="_"))

# Remove the prefix from all files inside that folder
new_folder_path <- file.path(paste(struc_result_dir,"/",paste(data_name,run_name,sep="_"), sep = ""))
files <- list.files(new_folder_path, full.names = TRUE)
file_names <- basename(files)
new_file_names <- sub(paste0("^", prefix_to_remove), "", file_names)
new_file_paths <- file.path(new_folder_path, new_file_names)
file.rename(files, new_file_paths)



### ANALYSIS - DETECT MIGRANTS

# For detecting recent migrants, import the data WITH related individuals
setwd(input_data_dir)
data_rel <- readRDS("BTF-3-155-14808-neutral-auto-genlight-sexinfo.RData")

data_rel2 <- gl.subsample.loci(data_rel, method = "random", mono.rm = TRUE, n = 5000)

# Check that these loci still capture the same structure in the data
data3 <- gl2gi(data_rel2) # convert to genind object
data3_scale <- scaleGen(data3,center = FALSE, scale = TRUE, NA.method = "mean")
pca1 <- dudi.pco(dist(data3_scale), scannf = FALSE,nf = 3)
pca1_df <- cbind(as.data.frame(pca1$li), data3$pop) # Colour points by location
colnames(pca1_df) <- c("Axis1","Axis2","Axis3","pop")
A <- ggplot(data = pca1_df, aes(x = Axis1, y = Axis2, color = pop))+
  stat_ellipse(geom = "path", aes(group = pop)) +
  geom_point(size = 1)+
  labs(x = paste("PC1 (",round((pca1$eig[1]/sum(pca1$eig))*100,digits=2),"%)", sep=""), y = paste("PC2 (",round((pca1$eig[2]/sum(pca1$eig))*100,digits=2),"%)", sep=""), color = "Region", title = "PC1 vs PC2")+
  guides(fill = guide_legend(byrow = TRUE))+
  #scale_color_manual(values = c("#2176ae","#ce4257","#1A936F")) +
  guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(linetype = "blank")))
A

# Save subset
setwd(input_data_dir)
saveRDS(data_rel2, "BTF-3-155-5000-neutral-auto-genlight-sexinfo.RData")

# Specify what data and run is being conducted for renaming the STRUCTURE output later
data_name <- "BTF-3-107-5000-rel"
run_name <- "STRUCTURE-gensback3-k3-run1" # Multiple runs performed, changing run # each time

# Set no. inferred pops
inferred_k <- 3

# Run STRUCTURE
setwd(struc_result_dir)
structure_result <- gl.run.structure(x = data_rel2, exec = structure_exec, plot.out = FALSE, delete.files = FALSE, k.range = inferred_k, num.k.rep = 4, burnin = 10000, numreps = 100000, noadmix = FALSE, freqscorr = TRUE, pop.prior = "usepopinfo", gensback = 3, migrprior = 0.05, pfrompopflagonly = FALSE, popflag = NULL, inferalpha = TRUE, alpha = 1)

# Define the prefix to remove
prefix_to_remove <- "gtypes.created.on.2024-06-13.15.59.14.structureRun" # changed as needed

# Rename the root folder where results were saved
setwd(struc_result_dir)
file.rename(prefix_to_remove, paste(data_name,run_name,sep="_"))

# Remove the prefix from all files inside that folder
new_folder_path <- file.path(paste(struc_result_dir,"/",paste(data_name,run_name,sep="_"), sep = ""))
files <- list.files(new_folder_path, full.names = TRUE)
file_names <- basename(files)
new_file_names <- sub(paste0("^", prefix_to_remove), "", file_names)
new_file_paths <- file.path(new_folder_path, new_file_names)
file.rename(files, new_file_paths)

```

```{r STRUCTURE - Plots & Evanno method}

# PLOT EVANNOT RESULTS

# Uses STRUCTURE HARVESTER (external to R) and the output files saved from gl.run.structure (you will need to modify the names so the name itself ends in _f with no extension, rather than ._f), then import the result 'evanno.txt' here. For STRUCTURE HARVESTER, download from: https://github.com/dentearl/structureHarvester/

# Set name to save plots
data_name <- "BTF-3-107-5000-unrel-STRUCTURE"

# Set path to STRUCTURE HARVESTER results. This assumes you already ran STRUCTURE HARVESTER and saved the results in the struc_result_dir directory in a sub-directory STRUHARV_results
setwd(paste0(struc_result_dir,"/STRUHARV_results"))

# Read in Evanno results
evanno_results <- read.table("evanno.txt")
colnames(evanno_results) <- c("K",	"Reps",	"Mean_LnP_K",	"Stdev_LnP_K",	"Ln_K",	"abs_Ln_K",	"Delta_K")
label_names <- c("K",	"Reps",	"Mean LnP(K)",	"Stdev LnP(K)",	"Ln'(K)",	"|Ln''(K)|",	"Delta K")
evanno_results

A <- ggplot(data = evanno_results, mapping = aes(x = factor(K), y = Mean_LnP_K))+
  geom_point()+
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = Mean_LnP_K - Stdev_LnP_K, ymax = Mean_LnP_K + Stdev_LnP_K), width = 0.2)+
  labs(x = "K", y = "Mean L(K)")+
  scale_x_discrete(labels = evanno_results$K)
A

B <- ggplot(data = evanno_results, mapping = aes(x = factor(K), y = Ln_K))+
  geom_point()+
  labs(x = "K", y = "Ln'(K)")+
  scale_x_discrete(labels = evanno_results$K)
B

C <- ggplot(data = evanno_results, mapping = aes(x = factor(K), y = abs_Ln_K))+
  geom_point()+
  labs(x = "K", y = "|Ln''(K)|")+
  scale_x_discrete(labels = evanno_results$K)
C

# Delta K is calculated as mean(|L''(K)|) / Stdev LnP(K)
D <- ggplot(data = evanno_results, aes(x = K, y = Delta_K)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_line() + # Add this line to connect the points
  labs(x = "K", y = "Delta K") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = evanno_results$K, labels = evanno_results$K)
D

# Save Evanno plots
plot_margin <- theme(plot.margin=margin(t = 10, r = 10, b = 5, l = 10, unit = "pt"))
plot_theme <- theme(panel.grid.major = element_blank(), panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), panel.background = element_rect(fill = NA), plot.background = element_rect(fill=NA), panel.border = element_rect(fill=NA),legend.position = "none") 
text_size <- theme(axis.title.x = element_text(size = 10), axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8, angle = 0, hjust = 0.5), axis.title.y = element_text(size = 10), axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),plot.title=element_text(size=11,face="bold",hjust=0),legend.text = element_text(size=6))

A2 <- A + plot_margin + plot_theme + text_size
B2 <- B + plot_margin + plot_theme + text_size
C2 <- C + plot_margin + plot_theme + text_size
D2 <- D + plot_margin + plot_theme + text_size

## TIFF and sized for Science Journal
setwd(paste0(struc_result_dir,"/_PLOTS"))
tiff(paste0(data_name,"-evanno.tif"),width = 17.5, height = 7, unit='cm', res=1200)
ggarrange(A2,D2,
          labels = c("A","B"),
          ncol = 2, nrow = 1)
dev.off()


### PLOT ADMIXTURE PLOTS

# K = 3

# Set working directory for where the CLUMPP result is saved. This assumes you saved the CLUMPP result in sub-folder CLUMPP_K3 - change for your own data.
setwd(paste0(struc_result_dir,"/STRUHARV_results/CLUMPP_K3"))
clump_result <- read.table(file = "K3.outfile", header=FALSE)
clump_result

# You'll also need the sample ids - get this from the original order of samples you used in STRUCTURE
setwd(paste0(struc_result_dir,"/STRUHARV_results"))
sample_order <- read.table(file = "sample_order.txt", header=FALSE)
sample_order <- sample_order$V1
sample_order

# Extract and rename the relevant columns (V6, V7, V8 corresponds to 3 genetic clusters - YOU NEED TO MODIFY THIS FOR YOUR SPECIFIC VALUE OF K)
clump_result2 <- clump_result[,c(1,6:8)] # select only the first and last three columns (for K=3, change for other values of K)
colnames(clump_result2) <- c("sample","cluster_1","cluster_2","cluster_3")
clump_result2

# Add sample names instead of ids (order will be the same)
clump_result2$sample <- sample_order
clump_result2

# Save updated dataframe with sample names
setwd(paste0(struc_result_dir,"/STRUHARV_results/CLUMPP_K3"))
write.csv(clump_result2,file = "K3-output-sample-names.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# Convert proportions to numeric
clump_result3 <- clump_result2 %>%
  mutate(cluster_1 = as.numeric(cluster_1),
         cluster_2 = as.numeric(cluster_2),
         cluster_3 = as.numeric(cluster_3))

# Melt the dataframe
clump_result4 <- clump_result3 %>%
  gather(key = "Cluster_props", value = "Value", -sample)
clump_result4

# Plot the stacked bar chart
E <- ggplot(clump_result4, aes(x = sample, y = Value, fill = Cluster_props)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  labs(x = "Sample", y = "Admixture coefficient", fill = "Cluster", title = "K = 3") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#26547c","#ef476f","#ffd166"), labels = c("1", "2", "3"))
E


# K = 4

# Input data
setwd(paste0(struc_result_dir,"/STRUHARV_results/CLUMPP_K4"))
clump_result <- read.table(file = "K4.outfile", header=FALSE)
clump_result

setwd(paste0(struc_result_dir,"/STRUHARV_results"))
sample_order <- read.table(file = "sample_order.txt", header=FALSE)
sample_order <- sample_order$V1
sample_order

# Extract and rename the relevant columns (V6, V7, V8 corresponds to 3 genetic clusters - YOU NEED TO MODIFY THIS FOR YOUR SPECIFIC VALUE OF K)
clump_result2 <- clump_result[,c(1,6:9)] # select only the first and last three columns (for K=3, change for other values of K)
colnames(clump_result2) <- c("sample","cluster_1","cluster_2","cluster_3","cluster_4")
clump_result2

# Add sample names instead of ids (order will be the same)
clump_result2$sample <- sample_order
clump_result2

setwd(paste0(struc_result_dir,"/STRUHARV_results/CLUMPP_K4"))
write.csv(clump_result2,file = "K4-output-sample-names.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# Convert proportions to numeric
clump_result3 <- clump_result2 %>%
  mutate(cluster_1 = as.numeric(cluster_1),
         cluster_2 = as.numeric(cluster_2),
         cluster_3 = as.numeric(cluster_3),
         cluster_4 = as.numeric(cluster_4))

# Melt the dataframe
clump_result4 <- clump_result3 %>%
  gather(key = "Cluster_props", value = "Value", -sample)
clump_result4

# Plot the stacked bar chart
F1 <- ggplot(clump_result4, aes(x = sample, y = Value, fill = Cluster_props)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  labs(x = "Sample", y = "Admixture coefficient", fill = "Cluster", title = "K = 4") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#26547c","#ef476f","#ffd166","#93e1d8"), labels = c("1", "2", "3","4"))
F1


# Save barplot with legend
plot_theme <- theme(panel.grid.major = element_blank(), panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), panel.background = element_rect(fill = NA), plot.background = element_rect(fill="white",color=NA), panel.border = element_rect(fill=NA),legend.position = "right") 
text_size <- theme(axis.title.x = element_text(size = 10), axis.text.x = element_text(size = 4.5, angle = 90, hjust = 0.5), axis.title.y = element_text(size = 10), axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),plot.title=element_text(size=11,face="bold",hjust=0),legend.text = element_text(size=6))

E2 <- E + plot_margin + plot_theme + text_size
F2 <- F1 + plot_margin + plot_theme + text_size

setwd(paste0(struc_result_dir,"/_PLOTS"))
tiff(paste0(data_name,"-barplot-K3-K4.tif"),width = 17.5, height = 12, unit='cm', res=1200)
ggarrange(E2,F2,
          labels = c("C","D"),
          ncol = 1, nrow = 2)
dev.off()

```

## 3.6 Stats

```{r Pairwise Fst heatmap}

# Load unrelated data
setwd(output_data_dir)
data <- readRDS("BTF-3-107-14788-neutral-auto-sex-unrel.RData")

# Set pop to sites
data@pop <- as.factor(data@other$ind.metrics$site_code)

# Exclude sites with n < 5
table(data$pop)
data <- gl.drop.pop(data, pop.list = c("BR","SA"), recalc = TRUE,mono.rm = TRUE)

# Order of pop names
mypoporder <- c("HPHT", "HPTT", "11MS", "10MB", "SWTM", "N2D", "3WFS", "4MB", "CMB", "LFT")
data@pop <- factor(data@pop, levels = mypoporder)

# Run with boots 
FST_boots <- gl.fst.pop(data, nboots = 1000, percent = 95)
FST_Fsts <- as.data.frame(FST_boots$Fsts)
min(FST_Fsts,na.rm=T)
max(FST_Fsts,na.rm=T)

FST_boots$Pvalues
FST_Ps <- as.data.frame(FST_boots$Pvalues)
min(FST_Ps,na.rm=T)
max(FST_Ps,na.rm=T)

# Convert the output to df with col1+2 as pops compared and col3 as FST
ind <- which(lower.tri(FST_Fsts, diag = FALSE), arr.ind = TRUE)
nn <- dimnames(FST_Fsts)
FST_Fsts_tab  <- data.frame(row = nn[[1]][ind[, 1]],
                                     col = nn[[2]][ind[, 2]],
                                     val = FST_Fsts[ind])
names(FST_Fsts_tab) <- c("Pop2","Pop1","FST")
# Also add a col for comparison number/row number so you can keep the order in the plot
# Adjust pop1 vs pop2 labelling,and fwd vs rev seq ordering to make heatmap ordered logically
FST_Fsts_tab$OrderPop1 <- seq(from=1, to=nrow(FST_Fsts_tab))
FST_Fsts_tab$OrderPop2 <- rev(seq(from=1, to=nrow(FST_Fsts_tab)))
head(FST_Fsts_tab)

# Save as .txt
setwd(stats_result_dir)
write.table(FST_Fsts_tab, "fst-for-Heatmap-unrelated.txt", row.names = F)

# Import data for plotting FST heatmap
setwd(stats_result_dir)
FSTtable<-read.table("fst-for-Heatmap-unrelated.txt", header=T)

# Plot fst heatmap
p1 <- ggplot(FSTtable, aes(x=reorder(Pop1,OrderPop1), y=reorder(Pop2, OrderPop2))) + 
  geom_tile(aes(fill = FST), colour = "white", stat="identity") + 
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "white", high = "#F8766D") +
  theme(panel.grid.major = element_blank(), 
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), 
        panel.background = element_blank(),
        axis.title.x=element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x=element_blank(),
        axis.title.y=element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.y=element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45,vjust=1,hjust=1),
        legend.background = element_rect(fill=NULL, linewidth=0.5, linetype="solid", colour ="lightgrey"),
        legend.position = c(0.9, 0.85))

# Save plot
setwd(paste0(stats_result_dir,"/_PLOTS"))
tiff('Pairwise_FST_Heatmap-sites-unrelated.tiff', 
     height=14, width=14, unit='cm', res=300)
p1
dev.off()  

```

```{r diveRsity stats - by region}

# Load unrelated data
setwd(output_data_dir)
data <- readRDS("BTF-3-107-14788-neutral-auto-sex-unrel.RData")
name <- "BTF-3-107-14788-neutral-auto-sex-unrel"
dataset <- "unrelated-regions"

# Save a copy of the genlight object in genepop format
gl2genepop(data,outfile=paste0(name,"-genepop.GEN"),outpath = output_data_dir) 

# Diversity stats by population (Ar, Fis, HWE, with bootstrapped CI's)
diveRsity_stats <- basicStats(infile = paste0(output_data_dir,"/",name,"-genepop.GEN"),outfile = paste0(output_dir_stats,"/","diveRsity-basicStats-",dataset), fis_ci = TRUE, ar_ci=TRUE, ar_boots = 999, fis_boots = 999, rarefaction = FALSE, fis_alpha = 0.05, mc_reps = 999)

# Save average stats overall by pop
diveRsity_pops <- names(diveRsity_stats$main_tab)
true_pops <- levels(data@pop)
num_pops <- length(true_pops)

# For pops in 1:num_pops, create a dataframe that adds on the summary stats results for each pop as an additional column
columns_list <- list()
columns_list[[1]] <- rownames(diveRsity_stats$main_tab[[1]])
for (i in 1:num_pops) {
  columns_list[[i + 1]] <- diveRsity_stats$main_tab[[i]][,(nLoc(data) + 1)]
}
stats_per_pop <- as.data.frame(do.call(cbind, columns_list))
colnames(stats_per_pop) <- c("stat", true_pops)
stats_per_pop

# Save results
setwd(stats_result_dir)
write.csv(stats_per_pop,file = paste0("diveRsity-basicStats-",dataset,"-summary.csv"), row.names = FALSE)


# Bootstrap Pairwise Fst [wont work with <3 pops]

# Set the output name
output_name <- paste0("diveRsity-",dataset)

# Run diffCalc with pairwise = TRUE and bs_pairwise = TRUE
setwd(stats_result_dir)
diveRsity_Fst <- diffCalc(infile = paste0(output_data_dir,"/",name,"-genepop.GEN"), outfile = output_name, fst = TRUE, pairwise = TRUE, bs_locus = FALSE, bs_pairwise = TRUE, boots = 100, ci_type = "individuals", alpha = 0.05)

# Extract pairwise Fst between pops with bs
pw_fst_result <- diveRsity_Fst$bs_pairwise$Fst
pw_fst_result$populations <- c("North-vs-Central","North-vs-South","Central-vs-South")

# Save result
setwd(paste0(stats_result_dir,"/","diveRsity-",dataset,"-[diffCalc]"))
write.csv(pw_fst_result,file = paste0("pairwise-Fst-summary.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

# Global Fstats (across all samples)
output_name <- paste0("diveRsity-global-",dataset)

setwd(stats_result_dir)
diveRsity_Fst_glb <- diffCalc(infile = paste0(output_data_dir,"/",name,"-genepop.GEN"), outfile = output_name, fst = TRUE, pairwise = FALSE, bs_locus = TRUE, bs_pairwise = FALSE, boots = 100, ci_type = "loci", alpha = 0.05)

# Extract average over all loci
glb_fst_result <- diveRsity_Fst_glb$global_bs

# Save summary result
setwd(paste0(stats_result_dir,"/","diveRsity-global-",dataset,"-[diffCalc]"))
write.csv(glb_fst_result,file = paste0("Global-stats-summary.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

```

```{r diveRsity stats - by sex}

# Load unrelated data
setwd(output_data_dir)
data <- readRDS("BTF-3-107-14788-neutral-auto-sex-unrel.RData")
name <- "BTF-3-107-14788-neutral-auto-sex-unrel"
dataset <- "unrelated-sex"

# Set pops to sex
data@pop <- as.factor(data@other$ind.metrics$sex)
table(data@pop)

# Save a copy of the genlight object in genepop format
gl2genepop(data,outfile=paste0(name,"-genepop.GEN"),outpath = output_data_dir) 

# Diversity stats by population (Ar, Fis, HWE, with bootstrapped CI's)
diveRsity_stats <- basicStats(infile = paste0(output_data_dir,"/",name,"-genepop.GEN"),outfile = paste0(output_dir_stats,"/","diveRsity-basicStats-",dataset), fis_ci = TRUE, ar_ci=TRUE, ar_boots = 999, fis_boots = 999, rarefaction = FALSE, fis_alpha = 0.05, mc_reps = 999)

# Save average stats overall by pop
diveRsity_pops <- names(diveRsity_stats$main_tab)
true_pops <- levels(data@pop)
num_pops <- length(true_pops)

# For pops in 1:num_pops, create a dataframe that adds on the summary stats results for each pop as an additional column
columns_list <- list() 
columns_list[[1]] <- rownames(diveRsity_stats$main_tab[[1]])
# Use a loop to add columns for each population
for (i in 1:num_pops) {
  columns_list[[i + 1]] <- diveRsity_stats$main_tab[[i]][,(nLoc(data) + 1)]
}
stats_per_pop <- as.data.frame(do.call(cbind, columns_list))
colnames(stats_per_pop) <- c("stat", true_pops)

# Save results
setwd(stats_result_dir)
write.csv(stats_per_pop,file = paste0("diveRsity-basicStats-",dataset,"-summary.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

```

## 3.7 Spatial analyses

```{r SAA - All regions}

# Load unrelated data
setwd(output_data_dir)
data <- readRDS("BTF-3-107-14788-neutral-auto-sex-unrel.RData")

# Merge pops
data2 <- gl.merge.pop(data, old = c("North","Central","South"), new = "AllSites")

# Examine distribution of euclidian distances between samples
dist_matrix <- distm(as.data.frame(cbind(data2$other$latlon$lon,data2$other$latlon$lat)))
dist_df <- data.frame(value = c(dist_matrix))

setwd(paste0(saa_result_dir,"/_PLOTS"))
jpeg("Histogram-euclidian-geo-distances.jpg", width = 500, height = 300)
hist(dist_df)
dev.off()

# Run SAA
spauto <- gl.spatial.autoCorr(data2,coordinates = "latlon",bins = c(0,2500,5000,10000,20000,30000,40000,50000,75110),reps = 9999,permutation = TRUE,bootstrap = TRUE)

# Format
spauto2 <- as.data.frame(spauto$AllSites)
spauto2$Bin_km <- spauto2$Bin / 1000

# Save the results
setwd(saa_result_dir)
write.csv(spauto2,file = "spauto_results-all-samples-unrelated.csv")

# Create scatterplot with error bars (error bars are the 95% confidence intervals around each value of 'r' calculated, based on bootstrapping)
A <- ggplot(spauto2, aes(x = Bin_km, y = r)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "solid", color = "grey")+
  geom_point() +
  geom_line() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = L.r, ymax = U.r)) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = spauto2$Bin_km)+
  labs(x = "Distance class (km)",y = "r (corrected)") +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = L.r.null, ymax = U.r.null), alpha = 0.3, fill = "#22908C") +
  geom_line(aes(y = L.r.null), linetype = "dashed", color = "#22908C") +
  geom_line(aes(y = U.r.null), linetype = "dashed", color = "#22908C")
A

# Save plot for publication

plot_margin <- theme(plot.margin=margin(t = 10, r = 10, b = 5, l = 10, unit = "pt"))
plot_theme <- theme(panel.grid.major = element_blank(), panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), panel.background = element_rect(fill = NA), plot.background = element_rect(fill=NA), panel.border = element_rect(fill=NA),legend.position = "none") 
text_size <- theme(axis.title.x = element_text(size = 10), axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8, angle = 45, hjust = 1), axis.title.y = element_text(size = 10), axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),plot.title=element_text(size=11,face="bold",hjust=0),legend.text = element_text(size=6))

A2 <- A + plot_margin + plot_theme + text_size

## TIFF and sized for Science Journal
setwd(paste0(saa_result_dir,"/_PLOTS"))
tiff("Spauto-all-samples-unrelated.tif",width = 12, height = 6, unit='cm', res=300)
ggarrange(A2,
          labels = c(""),
          ncol = 1, nrow = 1)
dev.off()

```

```{r SAA - Separately for each sex}

# Load unrelated data
setwd(output_data_dir)
data <- readRDS("BTF-3-107-14788-neutral-auto-sex-unrel.RData")

# FEMALES

data$pop <- as.factor(data@other$ind.metrics$sex)
data2 <- gl.keep.pop(data,pop.list = "F",recalc = TRUE,mono.rm = TRUE)

# Convert back to pop
data2$pop <- as.factor(data2@other$ind.metrics$pop)
table(data2$pop)
data3 <- gl.merge.pop(data2, old = c("North","Central","South"), new = "AllSites")

# Examine distribution of euclidian distances between samples
dist_matrix <- distm(as.data.frame(cbind(data3$other$latlon$lon,data3$other$latlon$lat)))
dist_df <- data.frame(value = c(dist_matrix))

setwd(paste0(saa_result_dir,"/_PLOTS"))
jpeg("Histogram-euclidian-geo-distances-females.jpg", width = 500, height = 300)
hist(dist_df)
dev.off()

# Run SAA
spauto <- gl.spatial.autoCorr(data3,coordinates = "latlon",bins = c(0,5000,10000,20000,30000,40000,76000),reps = 9999,permutation = TRUE,bootstrap = TRUE)

# Format results
spauto2 <- as.data.frame(spauto$AllSites)
spauto2$Bin_km <- spauto2$Bin / 1000

# Save the results
setwd(saa_result_dir)
write.csv(spauto2,file = "spauto_results-all-samples-unrelated-females.csv")

# create scatterplot with error bars (error bars are the 95% confidence intervals around each value of 'r' calculated, based on bootstrapping)
A <- ggplot(spauto2, aes(x = Bin_km, y = r)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "solid", color = "grey")+
  geom_point() +
  geom_line() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = L.r, ymax = U.r)) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = spauto2$Bin_km)+
  labs(x = "Distance class (km)",y = "r (corrected)", title = "Females") +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = L.r.null, ymax = U.r.null), alpha = 0.3, fill = "#FFCD00") +
  geom_line(aes(y = L.r.null), linetype = "dashed", color = "#FFCD00") +
  geom_line(aes(y = U.r.null), linetype = "dashed", color = "#FFCD00")
A


# MALES

data$pop <- as.factor(data@other$ind.metrics$sex)
data2 <- gl.keep.pop(data,pop.list = "M",recalc = TRUE,mono.rm = TRUE)

# Convert back to pop
data2$pop <- as.factor(data2@other$ind.metrics$pop)
table(data2$pop)
data3 <- gl.merge.pop(data2, old = c("North","Central","South"), new = "AllSites")

# Examine distribution of euclidian distances between samples
dist_matrix <- distm(as.data.frame(cbind(data3$other$latlon$lon,data3$other$latlon$lat)))
dist_df <- data.frame(value = c(dist_matrix))

setwd(paste0(saa_result_dir,"/_PLOTS"))
jpeg("Histogram-euclidian-geo-distances-males.jpg", width = 500, height = 300)
hist(dist_df)
dev.off()

# Run SAA
spauto <- gl.spatial.autoCorr(data3,coordinates = "latlon",bins = c(0,7000,20000,30000,40000,76000),reps = 9999,permutation = TRUE,bootstrap = TRUE)

# Format results
spauto2 <- as.data.frame(spauto$AllSites)
spauto2$Bin_km <- spauto2$Bin / 1000

# Save the results
setwd(saa_result_dir)
write.csv(spauto2,file = "spauto_results-all-samples-unrelated-males.csv")

# Create scatterplot with error bars (error bars are the 95% confidence intervals around each value of 'r' calculated, based on bootstrapping)
B <- ggplot(spauto2, aes(x = Bin_km, y = r)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "solid", color = "grey")+
  geom_point() +
  geom_line() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = L.r, ymax = U.r)) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = spauto2$Bin_km)+
  labs(x = "Distance class (km)",y = "r (corrected)", title = "Males") +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = L.r.null, ymax = U.r.null), alpha = 0.3, fill = "#00843D") +
  geom_line(aes(y = L.r.null), linetype = "dashed", color = "#00843D") +
  geom_line(aes(y = U.r.null), linetype = "dashed", color = "#00843D")
B


# Save plot for publication

plot_margin <- theme(plot.margin=margin(t = 10, r = 10, b = 5, l = 10, unit = "pt"))
plot_theme <- theme(panel.grid.major = element_blank(), panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), panel.background = element_rect(fill = NA), plot.background = element_rect(fill=NA), panel.border = element_rect(fill=NA),legend.position = "none") 
text_size <- theme(axis.title.x = element_text(size = 10), axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8, angle = 45, hjust = 1), axis.title.y = element_text(size = 10), axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),plot.title=element_text(size=11,face="bold",hjust=0),legend.text = element_text(size=6))

A2 <- A + plot_margin + plot_theme + text_size
B2 <- B + plot_margin + plot_theme + text_size

## TIFF and sized for Science Journal
setwd(paste0(saa_result_dir,"/_PLOTS"))
tiff("Spauto-all-samples-unrelated-MvsF.tif",width = 12, height = 12, unit='cm', res=1200)
ggarrange(A2,B2,
          labels = c("A","B"),
          ncol = 1, nrow = 2)
dev.off()


```

```{r SAA - North & Central regions only}

# Load unrelated data
setwd(output_data_dir)
data <- readRDS("BTF-3-107-14788-neutral-auto-sex-unrel.RData")

# Region = North-Central

data2 <- gl.merge.pop(data, old = c("North","Central"), new = "North-Central")
data3 <- gl.keep.pop(data2,pop.list = "North-Central",recalc = TRUE,mono.rm = TRUE)
pop_name1 <- "North-Central"

# Examine distribution of euclidian distances between samples
dist_matrix <- distm(as.data.frame(cbind(data3$other$latlon$lon,data3$other$latlon$lat)))
dist_df <- data.frame(value = c(dist_matrix))

setwd(paste0(saa_result_dir,"/_PLOTS"))
jpeg(paste("Histogram-euclidian-geo-distances-",pop_name1,".jpg",sep = ""), width = 500, height = 300)
hist(dist_df)
dev.off()

# Run SAA
spauto <- gl.spatial.autoCorr(data3,coordinates = "latlon",bins = c(0,2500,5000,10000,20000,35000,47000),reps = 9999,permutation = TRUE,bootstrap = TRUE)

# Format result
spauto2 <- as.data.frame(spauto$`North-Central`)
spauto2$Bin_km <- spauto2$Bin / 1000

# Save the results
setwd(saa_result_dir)
write.csv(spauto2,file = paste("Spauto-results-unrelated-",pop_name1,".csv",sep = ""))

# Create scatterplot with error bars (error bars are the 95% confidence intervals around each value of 'r' calculated, based on bootstrapping)
A <- ggplot(spauto2, aes(x = Bin_km, y = r)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "solid", color = "grey")+
  geom_point() +
  geom_line() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = L.r, ymax = U.r)) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = spauto2$Bin_km)+
  labs(x = "Distance class (km)",y = "r (corrected)",title = paste(pop_name1)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = L.r.null, ymax = U.r.null), alpha = 0.3, fill = "#22908C") +
  geom_line(aes(y = L.r.null), linetype = "dashed", color = "#22908C") +
  geom_line(aes(y = U.r.null), linetype = "dashed", color = "#22908C")
A



# Region = Central only

data3 <- gl.keep.pop(data,pop.list = "Central",recalc = TRUE,mono.rm = TRUE)
pop_name2 <- "Central"
              
# Examine distribution of euclidian distances between samples
dist_matrix <- distm(as.data.frame(cbind(data3$other$latlon$lon,data3$other$latlon$lat)))
dist_df <- data.frame(value = c(dist_matrix))

setwd(paste0(saa_result_dir,"/_PLOTS"))
jpeg(paste("Histogram-euclidian-geo-distances-",pop_name2,".jpg",sep = ""), width = 500, height = 300)
hist(dist_df)
dev.off()

# Run SAA
spauto <- gl.spatial.autoCorr(data3,coordinates = "latlon",bins = c(0,1000,3000,6000,9000,12000,16500),reps = 9999,permutation = TRUE,bootstrap = TRUE)

# Format results
spauto2 <- as.data.frame(spauto$`Central`)
spauto2$Bin_km <- spauto2$Bin / 1000

# Save the results
setwd(saa_result_dir)
write.csv(spauto2,file = paste("Spauto-results-unrelated-",pop_name2,".csv",sep = ""))

# Create scatterplot with error bars (error bars are the 95% confidence intervals around each value of 'r' calculated, based on bootstrapping)
B <- ggplot(spauto2, aes(x = Bin_km, y = r)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "solid", color = "grey")+
  geom_point() +
  geom_line() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = L.r, ymax = U.r)) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = spauto2$Bin_km)+
  labs(x = "Distance class (km)",y = "r (corrected)",title = paste(pop_name2)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = L.r.null, ymax = U.r.null), alpha = 0.3, fill = "#22908C") +
  geom_line(aes(y = L.r.null), linetype = "dashed", color = "#22908C") +
  geom_line(aes(y = U.r.null), linetype = "dashed", color = "#22908C")
B

# Save plot for publication

plot_margin <- theme(plot.margin=margin(t = 10, r = 10, b = 5, l = 10, unit = "pt"))
plot_theme <- theme(panel.grid.major = element_blank(), panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), panel.background = element_rect(fill = NA), plot.background = element_rect(fill=NA), panel.border = element_rect(fill=NA),legend.position = "none") 
text_size <- theme(axis.title.x = element_text(size = 10), axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8, angle = 45, hjust = 1), axis.title.y = element_text(size = 10), axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),plot.title=element_text(size=11,face="bold",hjust=0),legend.text = element_text(size=6))

A2 <- A + plot_margin + plot_theme + text_size
B2 <- B + plot_margin + plot_theme + text_size

## TIFF and sized for Science Journal
setwd(paste0(saa_result_dir,"/_PLOTS"))
tiff(paste("Spauto-unrelated-",pop_name1,".tif", sep=""),width = 12, height = 6, unit='cm', res=300)
ggarrange(A2,
          labels = c(""),
          ncol = 1, nrow = 1)
dev.off()
tiff(paste("Spauto-unrelated-",pop_name2,".tif", sep=""),width = 12, height = 6, unit='cm', res=300)
ggarrange(B2,
          labels = c(""),
          ncol = 1, nrow = 1)
dev.off()




# FEMALES

data$pop <- as.factor(data@other$ind.metrics$sex)
data2 <- gl.keep.pop(data,pop.list = "F",recalc = TRUE,mono.rm = TRUE)

# Convert back to pop and subset for target pop
data2$pop <- as.factor(data2@other$ind.metrics$pop)
data3 <- gl.keep.pop(data2,pop.list = "Central",recalc = TRUE,mono.rm = TRUE)
pop_name <- "Central"

# Examine distribution of euclidian distances between samples
dist_matrix <- distm(as.data.frame(cbind(data3$other$latlon$lon,data3$other$latlon$lat)))
dist_df <- data.frame(value = c(dist_matrix))

setwd(paste0(saa_result_dir,"/_PLOTS"))
jpeg(paste("Histogram-euclidian-geo-distances-females-",pop_name,".jpg",sep = ""), width = 500, height = 300)
hist(dist_df)
dev.off()

# Run SAA
spauto <- gl.spatial.autoCorr(data3,coordinates = "latlon",bins = c(0,2000,6000,9000,12000,16500),reps = 9999,permutation = TRUE,bootstrap = TRUE)

# Format results
spauto2 <- as.data.frame(spauto$Central)
spauto2$Bin_km <- spauto2$Bin / 1000

# Save the results
setwd(saa_result_dir)
write.csv(spauto2,file = paste("Spauto-results-unrelated-females-",pop_name,".csv",sep = ""))

# Create scatterplot with error bars (error bars are the 95% confidence intervals around each value of 'r' calculated, based on bootstrapping)
A <- ggplot(spauto2, aes(x = Bin_km, y = r)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "solid", color = "grey")+
  geom_point() +
  geom_line() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = L.r, ymax = U.r)) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = spauto2$Bin_km)+
  labs(x = "Distance class (km)",y = "r (corrected)",title = paste(pop_name,"females",sep=" ")) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = L.r.null, ymax = U.r.null), alpha = 0.3, fill = "#FFCD00") +
  geom_line(aes(y = L.r.null), linetype = "dashed", color = "#FFCD00") +
  geom_line(aes(y = U.r.null), linetype = "dashed", color = "#FFCD00")
A



# MALES

data$pop <- as.factor(data@other$ind.metrics$sex)
data2 <- gl.keep.pop(data,pop.list = "M",recalc = TRUE,mono.rm = TRUE)

# Convert back to pop and subset for target pop
data2$pop <- as.factor(data2@other$ind.metrics$pop)
data3 <- gl.keep.pop(data2,pop.list = "Central",recalc = TRUE,mono.rm = TRUE)
pop_name <- "Central"

# Examine distribution of euclidian distances between samples
dist_matrix <- distm(as.data.frame(cbind(data3$other$latlon$lon,data3$other$latlon$lat)))
dist_df <- data.frame(value = c(dist_matrix))

setwd(paste0(saa_result_dir,"/_PLOTS"))
jpeg(paste("Histogram-euclidian-geo-distances-males-",pop_name,".jpg",sep = ""), width = 500, height = 300)
hist(dist_df)
dev.off()

# Run SAA
spauto <- gl.spatial.autoCorr(data3,coordinates = "latlon",bins = c(0,2000,6000,9000,12000,16500),reps = 9999,permutation = TRUE,bootstrap = TRUE)

# Format results
spauto2 <- as.data.frame(spauto$Central)
spauto2$Bin_km <- spauto2$Bin / 1000

# Save the results
setwd(saa_result_dir)
write.csv(spauto2,file = paste("Spauto-results-unrelated-males-",pop_name,".csv",sep = ""))

# Create scatterplot with error bars (error bars are the 95% confidence intervals around each value of 'r' calculated, based on bootstrapping)
B <- ggplot(spauto2, aes(x = Bin_km, y = r)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "solid", color = "grey")+
  geom_point() +
  geom_line() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = L.r, ymax = U.r)) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = spauto2$Bin_km)+
  labs(x = "Distance class (km)",y = "r (corrected)",title = paste(pop_name,"males",sep=" ")) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = L.r.null, ymax = U.r.null), alpha = 0.3, fill = "#00843D") +
  geom_line(aes(y = L.r.null), linetype = "dashed", color = "#00843D") +
  geom_line(aes(y = U.r.null), linetype = "dashed", color = "#00843D")
B

# Save plot for publication

plot_margin <- theme(plot.margin=margin(t = 10, r = 10, b = 5, l = 10, unit = "pt"))
plot_theme <- theme(panel.grid.major = element_blank(), panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), panel.background = element_rect(fill = NA), plot.background = element_rect(fill=NA), panel.border = element_rect(fill=NA),legend.position = "none") 
text_size <- theme(axis.title.x = element_text(size = 10), axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8, angle = 45, hjust = 1), axis.title.y = element_text(size = 10), axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),plot.title=element_text(size=11,face="bold",hjust=0),legend.text = element_text(size=6))

A2 <- A + plot_margin + plot_theme + text_size
B2 <- B + plot_margin + plot_theme + text_size

## TIFF and sized for Science Journal
setwd(paste0(saa_result_dir,"/_PLOTS"))
tiff("Spauto-all-samples-unrelated-MvsF-CentralPop.tif",width = 12, height = 12, unit='cm', res=1200)
ggarrange(A2,B2,
          labels = c("A","B"),
          ncol = 1, nrow = 2)
dev.off()


```

```{r SAA for Appendix}

# All samples plot
setwd(saa_result_dir)
spauto2 <- read.csv(file = "spauto_results-all-samples-unrelated.csv", header = TRUE)

# create scatterplot with error bars (error bars are the 95% confidence intervals around each value of 'r' calculated, based on bootstrapping)
A <- ggplot(spauto2, aes(x = Bin_km, y = r)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "solid", color = "grey")+
  geom_point() +
  geom_line() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = L.r, ymax = U.r)) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = spauto2$Bin_km)+
  labs(x = "Distance class (km)",y = "r (corrected)", title = "All regions") + 
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = L.r.null, ymax = U.r.null), alpha = 0.3, fill = "grey30") +
  geom_line(aes(y = L.r.null), linetype = "dashed", color = "grey30") +
  geom_line(aes(y = U.r.null), linetype = "dashed", color = "grey30")
A

# All samples Females plot
setwd(saa_result_dir)
spauto2 <- read.csv(file = "spauto_results-all-samples-unrelated-females.csv", header = TRUE)

B <- ggplot(spauto2, aes(x = Bin_km, y = r)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "solid", color = "grey")+
  geom_point() +
  geom_line() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = L.r, ymax = U.r)) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = spauto2$Bin_km)+
  labs(x = "Distance class (km)",y = "r (corrected)", title = "Females") + 
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = L.r.null, ymax = U.r.null), alpha = 0.3, fill = "#FFCD00") +
  geom_line(aes(y = L.r.null), linetype = "dashed", color = "#FFCD00") +
  geom_line(aes(y = U.r.null), linetype = "dashed", color = "#FFCD00")
B

# All samples Males plot
setwd(saa_result_dir)
spauto2 <- read.csv(file = "spauto_results-all-samples-unrelated-males.csv", header = TRUE)

C <- ggplot(spauto2, aes(x = Bin_km, y = r)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "solid", color = "grey")+
  geom_point() +
  geom_line() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = L.r, ymax = U.r)) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = spauto2$Bin_km)+
  labs(x = "Distance class (km)",y = "r (corrected)", title = "Males") + 
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = L.r.null, ymax = U.r.null), alpha = 0.3, fill = "#00843D") +
  geom_line(aes(y = L.r.null), linetype = "dashed", color = "#00843D") +
  geom_line(aes(y = U.r.null), linetype = "dashed", color = "#00843D")
C


# Central only plot
setwd(saa_result_dir)
spauto2 <- read.csv(file = "spauto-results-unrelated-Central.csv", header = TRUE)

# create scatterplot with error bars (error bars are the 95% confidence intervals around each value of 'r' calculated, based on bootstrapping)
D <- ggplot(spauto2, aes(x = Bin_km, y = r)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "solid", color = "grey")+
  geom_point() +
  geom_line() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = L.r, ymax = U.r)) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = spauto2$Bin_km)+
  labs(x = "Distance class (km)",y = "r (corrected)", title = "Central region") + 
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = L.r.null, ymax = U.r.null), alpha = 0.3, fill = "grey30") +
  geom_line(aes(y = L.r.null), linetype = "dashed", color = "grey30") +
  geom_line(aes(y = U.r.null), linetype = "dashed", color = "grey30")
D

# Central Females plot
setwd(saa_result_dir)
spauto2 <- read.csv(file = "spauto-results-unrelated-females-Central.csv", header = TRUE)

E <- ggplot(spauto2, aes(x = Bin_km, y = r)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "solid", color = "grey")+
  geom_point() +
  geom_line() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = L.r, ymax = U.r)) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = spauto2$Bin_km)+
  labs(x = "Distance class (km)",y = "r (corrected)", title = "Females") + 
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = L.r.null, ymax = U.r.null), alpha = 0.3, fill = "#FFCD00") +
  geom_line(aes(y = L.r.null), linetype = "dashed", color = "#FFCD00") +
  geom_line(aes(y = U.r.null), linetype = "dashed", color = "#FFCD00")
E

# Central Males plot
setwd(saa_result_dir)
spauto2 <- read.csv(file = "spauto-results-unrelated-males-Central.csv", header = TRUE)

F1 <- ggplot(spauto2, aes(x = Bin_km, y = r)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "solid", color = "grey")+
  geom_point() +
  geom_line() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = L.r, ymax = U.r)) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = spauto2$Bin_km)+
  labs(x = "Distance class (km)",y = "r (corrected)", title = "Males") + 
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = L.r.null, ymax = U.r.null), alpha = 0.3, fill = "#00843D") +
  geom_line(aes(y = L.r.null), linetype = "dashed", color = "#00843D") +
```
