```{r SAA for Appendix}
  geom_line(aes(y = U.r.null), linetype = "dashed", color = "#00843D")
F1


# Save plot for publication

plot_margin <- theme(plot.margin=margin(t = 10, r = 10, b = 5, l = 10, unit = "pt"))
plot_theme <- theme(panel.grid.major = element_blank(), panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), panel.background = element_rect(fill = NA), plot.background = element_rect(fill=NA), panel.border = element_rect(fill=NA),legend.position = "none") 
text_size <- theme(axis.title.x = element_text(size = 10), axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8, angle = 45, hjust = 1), axis.title.y = element_text(size = 10), axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),plot.title=element_text(size=11,face="bold",hjust=0),legend.text = element_text(size=6))

A2 <- A + plot_margin + plot_theme + text_size
B2 <- B + plot_margin + plot_theme + text_size
C2 <- C + plot_margin + plot_theme + text_size
D2 <- D + plot_margin + plot_theme + text_size
E2 <- E + plot_margin + plot_theme + text_size
F2 <- F1 + plot_margin + plot_theme + text_size

## TIFF and sized for Science Journal
setwd(paste0(saa_result_dir,"/_PLOTS"))
tiff("BTF-SAA-for-Appendix.tif",width = 17.5, height = 18, unit='cm', res=300)
ggarrange(A2,D2,B2,E2,C2,F2,
          labels = c("A","B","","","",""),
          ncol = 2, nrow = 3)
dev.off()


```

## 3.8 Spatial Principal Components Analysis (sPCA)

```{r sPCA}

# Load unrelated data
setwd(output_data_dir)
data <- readRDS("BTF-3-107-14788-neutral-auto-sex-unrel.RData")

# check no. samples per site
table(data$pop)

# Random subset of snps to reduce computation time
data <- gl.subsample.loci(data,n = 5000,method = "random",mono.rm = TRUE)
data2 <- gl2gi(data)

# Swap latlong to longlat
latlong <- data2$other$latlon
longlat <- as.data.frame(cbind(latlong$lon,latlong$lat))
colnames(longlat) <- c("lon","lat")
rownames(longlat) <- rownames(latlong)

# Add minor random noise to the latitude and longitude values (for mapping purposes, otherwise interpolations won't work later)
longlat$lon <- jitter(longlat$lon, factor = 0.1)
longlat$lat <- jitter(longlat$lat, factor = 0.1)
data2$other$latlon <- longlat

# Save the genind object
setwd(spca_result_dir)
saveRDS(data2, "BTF-3-107-5000-neutral-unrel-subset.RData")

### ANALYSIS

# Mantel test
data_norm <- scaleGen(data2,center = TRUE, scale = TRUE, NA.method = "mean")
mtest <- mantel.randtest(dist(data_norm), dist(data2$other$latlon))

plot(mtest, nclass=30)
plot(dist(data2$other$latlon),dist(data_norm))

setwd(spca_result_dir)
sink(file = "Mantel-test-unrel.txt")
cat(print(mtest))
sink()


# sPCA type 7
# All sites are neighbours, but the spatial weights are directly proportional to the inversed spatial distances). a = exponent used for type 7. dmin = the minimum distance between any two distinct points. Used to avoid infinite spatial proximities. We retain the top 10 global and top 10 local eigenvectors, but you can set scannf to TRUE and ask to TRUE to choose these yourself based on the graph
mySpca <- adegenet::spca(obj=data2,xy = data2@other$latlon, ask = FALSE, type = 7, a = 2, dmin = 0, scannf=FALSE, nfposi = 10, nfnega = 10)

# Save the sPCA object
setwd(spca_result_dir)
saveRDS(mySpca, file="sPCA-object-type7-exp2-unrel-5000loci.rds")

# Import sPCA object
setwd(spca_result_dir)
mySpca <- readRDS("sPCA-object-type7-exp2-unrel-5000loci.rds")
name <- "BTF-3-107-5000-neutral-unrel"
type <- "Type7"

# Import the saved genind object that was used to run the sPCA
setwd(spca_result_dir)
data2 <- readRDS("BTF-3-107-5000-neutral-unrel-subset.RData")

# Save the results of the sPCA
results <- summary(mySpca) # Takes around 30 min for 5000 loci
setwd(spca_result_dir)
write.csv(results$pca, file = paste(name,type,"results-pca.csv",sep="-"))
write.csv(results$spca, file = paste(name,type,"results-spca.csv",sep="-"))
write.csv(mySpca$as, file = paste(name,type,"sPCA-vs-PCA-axes.csv",sep="-"))
sink(file = paste(name,type,"results-Istat.txt",sep="-"))
cat(print(results$Istat))
sink()

# Screeplot - plot variance vs I for each eigenvalue.
setwd(paste0(spca_result_dir,"/_PLOTS"))
jpeg(filename = paste(name,type,"screeplot.jpg",sep="-"))
screeplot(mySpca) # Takes around 30 min for 5000 loci
dev.off()

# Multi plot
setwd(paste0(spca_result_dir,"/_PLOTS"))
jpeg(filename = paste(name,type,"multiplot.jpg",sep="-"),width = 600, height = 800)
plot(mySpca)
dev.off()

# Save the global and local stats tests
data_norm <- scaleGen(data2,center = TRUE, scale = TRUE, NA.method = "mean")
myGtest <- global.rtest(data_norm,mySpca$lw,nperm=999)
myLtest <- local.rtest(data_norm,mySpca$lw,nperm=999)

setwd(spca_result_dir)
sink(paste(name,type,"results-Gtest.txt",sep="-"))
cat(print(myGtest))
sink()
sink(paste(name,type,"results-Ltest.txt",sep="-"))
cat(print(myLtest))
sink()

```

```{r sPCA plot for Appendix}

# Import sPCA object
setwd(spca_result_dir)
mySpca <- readRDS("sPCA-object-type7-exp2-unrel-5000loci.rds")
name <- "BTF-3-107-5000-neutral-unrel"
type <- "Type7"

# Import the saved genind object that was used to run the sPCA
setwd(spca_result_dir)
data2 <- readRDS("BTF-3-107-5000-neutral-unrel-subset.RData")
data2

# Mapping shapefile
map_dir <- paste0(getwd(),"/figures/Fig1-combined-map-structure/Aus_States")

# Plot eigenvalues by their I and variance
setwd(spca_result_dir)
scree_data <- read.csv(file = paste(name,type,"results-spca.csv",sep="-"), header = TRUE)

n_colors <- length(scree_data$eig)
color_palette <- colorRampPalette(brewer.pal(n_colors, "RdBu"))

A <- ggplot(data = scree_data, aes(x = var, y = moran, color = eig))+
  geom_point(size = 1)+
  scale_color_gradientn(colors = color_palette(n_colors))+
  labs(x = "Variance", y = expression(paste("Moran's ", italic(I))))+
  geom_point(data = scree_data, aes(x = var[1], y = moran[1]),color = "black", pch = 1, size = 3)
A

# Plot of eigenvalues in order of most positive to most negative

B <- ggplot(data = scree_data, aes(x = 1:nrow(scree_data), y = eig)) +
  geom_linerange(aes(ymin = 0, ymax = eig, color = eig), size = 0.5) +
  scale_color_gradientn(colors = color_palette(n_colors), na.value = "grey50") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, col = "grey") +
  labs(x = "Eigenvector", y = "Score")
B

# Map of interpolated ls (lagged scores). Using the lagged principal scores (ls) rather than the original scores (li) gives better clarity of genetic clines.

# Load the shapefile
setwd(map_dir)
aus_basins_map <- readOGR("georef-australia-state-millesime.shp")
g_fort = fortify(aus_basins_map)

x <- data2$other$latlon[,1]
y <- data2$other$latlon[,2]

interpX <- seq(min(x),max(x),le=200)
interpY <- seq(min(y),max(y),le=200)
gdat_interp <- interp(x, y, mySpca$ls[,1], xo=interpX, yo=interpY)
gdat <- interp2xyz(gdat_interp, data.frame = TRUE)
gdat

# For plotting min and max x and y on the map plot, set the limits based on the lat and long coordinates, but add a small margin
margin_x <- (max(x) - min(x)) * 0.60  # e.g. 0.05 = 5% of the range of x
margin_y <- (max(y) - min(y)) * 0.05  # e.g. 0.05 = 5% of the range of y

# Create a ggplot map and add the points of samples based on longitude and latitude
C = ggplot() +
  geom_polygon(data=g_fort,aes(x=long,y=lat,group=group), fill='transparent',color = "black")+
  coord_equal()+
  geom_tile(data = gdat, aes(x = x, y = y, z = z, fill = z))+
  geom_contour(data = gdat, aes(x = x, y = y, z = z), color = "white", alpha = 0.5) + 
  scale_fill_distiller(palette="Spectral", na.value=alpha("white", alpha=0))+
  geom_polygon(data=g_fort,aes(x=long,y=lat,group=group), fill='transparent',color = "gray40")+
  geom_point(data = data2$other$latlon, aes(x = lon, y = lat), color = "black", size = 1)+
  labs(x = "Longitude", y = "Latitude")+
  xlim(min(x) - margin_x, max(x) + margin_x) + # remove this if you want to plot all of Australia
  ylim(min(y) - margin_y, max(y) + margin_y) # remove this if you want to plot all of Australia
C

# Save plot for publication

plot_margin <- theme(plot.margin=margin(t = 10, r = 10, b = 5, l = 10, unit = "pt"))
plot_theme <- theme(panel.grid.major = element_blank(), panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), panel.background = element_rect(fill = NA), plot.background = element_rect(fill=NA), panel.border = element_rect(fill=NA),legend.position = "none") 
text_size <- theme(axis.title.x = element_text(size = 10), axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8,angle = 45, hjust = 1), axis.title.y = element_text(size = 10), axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),plot.title=element_text(size=11,face="bold",hjust=0),legend.text = element_text(size=6))

A2 <- A + plot_margin + plot_theme + text_size
B2 <- B + plot_margin + plot_theme + text_size
C2 <- C + plot_margin + plot_theme + text_size

setwd(paste0(spca_result_dir,"/_PLOTS"))
tiff("Neutral-sPCA-type7-unrelated-plotA.tif",width = 5.5, height = 10, unit='cm', res=1200)
ggarrange(C2,
          labels = c("B"),
          ncol = 1, nrow = 1)
dev.off()

setwd(paste0(spca_result_dir,"/_PLOTS"))
tiff("Neutral-sPCA-type7-unrelated-plotB-C.tif",width = 7.5, height = 10, unit='cm', res=1200)
ggarrange(A2,B2,
          labels = c("C","D"),
          ncol = 1, nrow = 2)
dev.off()

```

## 3.9 Sex-biased dispersal tests

```{r Sex bias dispersal tests}

# Load unrelated data
setwd(output_data_dir)
data <- readRDS("BTF-3-107-14788-neutral-auto-sex-unrel.RData")

### REGIONS AS 'POPS'

# Exclude samples with inconsistent or uncertain sex results
data$pop <- as.factor(data$other$ind.metrics$sex)
data2 <- gl.keep.pop(data, pop.list = c("F","M"),recalc = TRUE,mono.rm = TRUE)

# Check samples sizes
data_males <- gl.keep.pop(data2,pop.list = "M",recalc = TRUE,mono.rm = TRUE)
data_males$pop <- as.factor(data_males$other$ind.metrics$pop)
table(data_males$pop)
data_females <- gl.keep.pop(data2,pop.list = "F",recalc = TRUE,mono.rm = TRUE)
data_females$pop <- as.factor(data_females$other$ind.metrics$pop)
table(data_females$pop)

# Convert pop back to region for full dataset
data2$pop <- as.factor(data2@other$ind.metrics$pop)

# Convert to hierfstat format
data_geno2 <- genind2hierfstat(gl2gi(data2))

# Extract sex from the original genlight object
data_sex <- data2$other$ind.metrics$sex

# Confirm order is the same for individuals
rownames(data_geno2) == data2$other$ind.metrics$id

# Corrected mean assignment indexes
aic<-AIc(data_geno2)
boxplot(aic~data_sex)

# Convert AIc to dataframe
aic_df <- as.data.frame(aic)
aic_df <- cbind(rownames(data_geno2),data_sex,aic_df)
colnames(aic_df) <- c("id","sex","aic")

# Save the dataframe
setwd(sexbias_result_dir)
write.csv(aic_df, file = "sexbiastests-aicdf-unrel-regions.csv",row.names = FALSE)

# Sex-biased test two-sided tests
maic_test <- sexbias.test(data_geno2,data_sex,nperm = 100,test = "mAIc",alternative = "two.sided")
vaic_test <- sexbias.test(data_geno2,data_sex,nperm = 100,test = "vAIc",alternative = "two.sided")
fis_test <- sexbias.test(data_geno2,data_sex,nperm = 100,test = "FIS",alternative = "two.sided")
fst_test <- sexbias.test(data_geno2,data_sex,nperm = 100,test = "FST",alternative = "two.sided")

# Save results
setwd(sexbias_result_dir)
sink("sexbiastests-two-sided-results-unrel-regions.txt")
cat("Sample sizes\n\n")
print(table(data2$pop))
cat("\n")
print(table(data2$other$ind.metrics$sex))
cat("\n\n")
cat("mAIc\n\n")
print(maic_test)
cat("\n")
cat("vAIc\n\n")
print(vaic_test)
cat("\n")
cat("Fis\n\n")
print(fis_test)
cat("\n")
cat("Fst\n\n")
print(fst_test)
cat("\n")
sink()


### SITES AS 'POP'

# Check sample sizes per site for each sex
data_males$pop <- as.factor(data_males$other$ind.metrics$site_code)
table(data_males$pop)
data_females$pop <- as.factor(data_females$other$ind.metrics$site_code)
table(data_females$pop)

# Set pop to site locations
data2$pop <- as.factor(data2@other$ind.metrics$site_code)

# Remove any sites that have <2 males or <2 females, and any sites that only have one sex, using gl.drop.pop or gl.keep.pop
data3 <- gl.drop.pop(data2, pop.list = c("BR","SA","SWTM"),recalc = TRUE,mono.rm = TRUE)

# Final number of males and females for this test
table(data3$other$ind.metrics$sex)

# Convert to hierfstat format
data_geno2 <- genind2hierfstat(gl2gi(data3))

# Extract sex from the original genlight object
data_sex <- data3$other$ind.metrics$sex

# Confirm order is the same for individuals
rownames(data_geno2) == data3$other$ind.metrics$id

# Samples per sex per site
table(data3$pop, data3$other$ind.metrics$sex)

# Corrected mean assignment indexes
aic<-AIc(data_geno2) # This can take a while for large datasets
boxplot(aic~data_sex)

# Convert AIc to dataframe
aic_df <- as.data.frame(aic)
aic_df <- cbind(rownames(data_geno2),data_sex,aic_df)
colnames(aic_df) <- c("id","sex","aic")

# Save the dataframe
setwd(sexbias_result_dir)
write.csv(aic_df, file = "sexbiastests-aicdf-unrel-sites.csv",row.names = FALSE)

# Sex-biased test two-sided tests
maic_test <- sexbias.test(data_geno2,data_sex,nperm = 100,test = "mAIc",alternative = "two.sided")
vaic_test <- sexbias.test(data_geno2,data_sex,nperm = 100,test = "vAIc",alternative = "two.sided")
fis_test <- sexbias.test(data_geno2,data_sex,nperm = 100,test = "FIS",alternative = "two.sided")
fst_test <- sexbias.test(data_geno2,data_sex,nperm = 100,test = "FST",alternative = "two.sided")

# Save results
setwd(sexbias_result_dir)
sink("sexbiastests-two-sided-results-unrel-sites.txt")
cat("Sample sizes\n\n")
print(table(data3$pop))
cat("\n")
print(table(data3$other$ind.metrics$sex))
cat("\n\n")
cat("mAIc\n\n")
print(maic_test)
cat("\n")
cat("vAIc\n\n")
print(vaic_test)
cat("\n")
cat("Fis\n\n")
print(fis_test)
cat("\n")
cat("Fst\n\n")
print(fst_test)
cat("\n")
sink()

```

## 3.10 Kinship

```{r Kinship - format kinship data}

# Load unrelated data
setwd(output_data_dir)
data <- readRDS("BTF-3-107-14788-neutral-auto-sex-unrel.RData")

# Read in the updated kinship data from the PC-AiR/PCRelate analysis, accounting for genetic structure.
setwd(pcairelate_result_dir)
kin_df <- read.csv("PCRelate-updated-kinship-dataframe-iteration2.csv",header = TRUE)

# Read in list of 'unrelated' samples
setwd(pcairelate_result_dir)
unrel_metadata <- read.csv("BTF-final-samples-metadata-unrelated.csv", header = TRUE)
unrel_samples <- unrel_metadata$id

# Remove highly related samples from kin_df
kin_df2 <- kin_df %>% filter(ID1 %in% unrel_samples & ID2 %in% unrel_samples)

# Add pairwise sample metadata to the kinship dataframe

# Get the sample metadata from the genlight object
meta_data <- data$other$ind.metrics

# For each pair of samples, if the 'site_code' column matches, then write 'Within_sites' in the new column 'Kinship_Site_Label', but if not write 'Between_sites'
site_a <- meta_data$site_code[match(kin_df2$ID1, meta_data$id)] # Get the Site values for sample A and sample B in meta_data
site_b <- meta_data$site_code[match(kin_df2$ID2, meta_data$id)]
kin_df2$Kinship_Site_Label <- ifelse(site_a == site_b, "Within-sites", "Between-sites") # Create a new column in kin_df2 based on the Site values

# Repeat for regions
region_a <- meta_data$pop[match(kin_df2$ID1, meta_data$id)]
region_b <- meta_data$pop[match(kin_df2$ID2, meta_data$id)]
kin_df2$Kinship_Region_Label <- ifelse(region_a == region_b, "Within-regions", "Between-regions")

# More specific region labels
kin_df2$Kinship_Region2_Label <- ifelse(
  region_a == "North" & region_b == "North", "North", 
  ifelse(region_a == "South" & region_b == "South", "South",
         ifelse(region_a == "Central" & region_b == "Central", "Central", "Between-regions")))

# Add sex information to the kinship dataframe
sex_a <- meta_data$sex[match(kin_df2$ID1, meta_data$id)]
sex_b <- meta_data$sex[match(kin_df2$ID2, meta_data$id)]
kin_df2$Kinship_Sex_Label <- ifelse(
  sex_a == "F" & sex_b == "F", "Between-females", 
  ifelse(sex_a == "M" & sex_b == "M", "Between-males", "Between-sexes"))

# Add Survey info
survey_a <- meta_data$survey[match(kin_df2$ID1, meta_data$id)]
survey_b <- meta_data$survey[match(kin_df2$ID2, meta_data$id)]
kin_df2$Kinship_Survey_Label <- ifelse(survey_a == survey_b, "Within-surveys", "Between-surveys")

# Add Season info
season_a <- meta_data$season[match(kin_df2$ID1, meta_data$id)]
season_b <- meta_data$season[match(kin_df2$ID2, meta_data$id)]
kin_df2$Kinship_Season_Label <- ifelse(season_a == season_b, "Within-seasons", "Between-seasons")

# Add specific season labels (MODIFY THIS TO SUIT YOUR OWN SPECIFIC POPS OR REGIONS)
kin_df2$Kinship_Season2_Label <- ifelse(
  season_a == "Dry" & season_b == "Dry", "Dry", 
  ifelse(season_a == "Early Wet" & season_b == "Early Wet", "Early Wet",
         ifelse(season_a == "Wet" & season_b == "Wet", "Wet", "Between-seasons")))

# Plot mean and 95% Confidence intervals
A <- ggplot(kin_df2, aes(x = Kinship_Site_Label, y = kin, color = Kinship_Site_Label)) +
  stat_summary(fun.data = "mean_cl_normal", geom = "errorbar", width = 0.2) + # Add a mean layer with 95% confidence intervals
  stat_summary(fun.y = "mean", geom = "point", size = 3, shape = 20, fill = "white") + # Add a point layer for the mean value
  labs(x = "Group", y = "Mean kinship") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("red", "blue"), name = "Group")   # change colours of groups
A

B <- ggplot(kin_df2, aes(x = Kinship_Region_Label, y = kin, color = Kinship_Region_Label)) +
  stat_summary(fun.data = "mean_cl_normal", geom = "errorbar", width = 0.2) + # Add a mean layer with 95% confidence intervals
  stat_summary(fun.y = "mean", geom = "point", size = 3, shape = 20, fill = "white") + # Add a point layer for the mean value
  labs(x = "Group", y = "Mean kinship") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("red", "blue"), name = "Group")   # change colours of groups
B

C <- ggplot(kin_df2, aes(x = Kinship_Sex_Label, y = kin, color = Kinship_Sex_Label)) +
  stat_summary(fun.data = "mean_cl_normal", geom = "errorbar", width = 0.2) + # Add a mean layer with 95% confidence intervals
  stat_summary(fun.y = "mean", geom = "point", size = 3, shape = 20, fill = "white") + # Add a point layer for the mean value
  labs(x = "Group", y = "Mean kinship") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("red", "blue","green"), name = "Group")   # change colours of groups
C

D <- ggplot(kin_df2, aes(x = Kinship_Sex_Label, y = kin, color = Kinship_Sex_Label)) +
  stat_summary(fun.data = "mean_cl_normal", geom = "errorbar", width = 0.2) + # Add a mean layer with 95% confidence intervals
  stat_summary(fun.y = "mean", geom = "point", size = 3, shape = 20, fill = "white") + # Add a point layer for the mean value
  labs(x = "Group", y = "Mean kinship") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("red", "blue","green"), name = "Group")+
  facet_grid(~ Kinship_Region2_Label)
D

E <- ggplot(kin_df2, aes(x = Kinship_Survey_Label, y = kin, color = Kinship_Survey_Label)) +
  stat_summary(fun.data = "mean_cl_normal", geom = "errorbar", width = 0.2) + # Add a mean layer with 95% confidence intervals
  stat_summary(fun.y = "mean", geom = "point", size = 3, shape = 20, fill = "white") + # Add a point layer for the mean value
  labs(x = "Group", y = "Mean kinship") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("red", "blue"), name = "Group")   # change colours of groups
E

F1 <- ggplot(kin_df2, aes(x = Kinship_Season_Label, y = kin, color = Kinship_Season_Label)) +
  stat_summary(fun.data = "mean_cl_normal", geom = "errorbar", width = 0.2) + # Add a mean layer with 95% confidence intervals
  stat_summary(fun.y = "mean", geom = "point", size = 3, shape = 20, fill = "white") + # Add a point layer for the mean value
  labs(x = "Group", y = "Mean kinship") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("red", "blue"), name = "Group")   # change colours of groups
F1

G <- ggplot(kin_df2, aes(x = Kinship_Season2_Label, y = kin, color = Kinship_Season2_Label)) +
  stat_summary(fun.data = "mean_cl_normal", geom = "errorbar", width = 0.2) + # Add a mean layer with 95% confidence intervals
  stat_summary(fun.y = "mean", geom = "point", size = 3, shape = 20, fill = "white") + # Add a point layer for the mean value
  labs(x = "Group", y = "Mean kinship") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("red", "blue","green","yellow"), name = "Group")+
  facet_grid(~ Kinship_Region2_Label)
G

# Add pairwise geographic distance to the kinship dataframe

# Get lat long dataframe
df <- data$other$latlon

# Create an empty dataframe to store the results
geodist_df <- data.frame(Sample1 = character(), Sample2 = character(), Distance = numeric(), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# Get all combinations of row indices
row_combinations <- combn(nrow(df), 2)

# Iterate through each combination and calculate the distance
for (i in 1:ncol(row_combinations)) {
  sample1 <- rownames(df)[row_combinations[1, i]]
  sample2 <- rownames(df)[row_combinations[2, i]]
  coords1 <- c(df[sample1, "lon"], df[sample1, "lat"])
  coords2 <- c(df[sample2, "lon"], df[sample2, "lat"])
  distance <- distGeo(coords1, coords2)
  
  # Add the results to the dataframe
  geodist_df <- rbind(geodist_df, data.frame(Sample1 = sample1, Sample2 = sample2, Distance = distance))
}

# Change from meters to kilometers
geodist_df$Distance_km <- round((geodist_df$Distance/1000),3)

# Merge geodist_df and kin_data based on sample IDs
kin_df3 <- merge(geodist_df, kin_df2, by.x = c("Sample1", "Sample2"), by.y = c("ID1", "ID2"))

D <- ggplot(data = kin_df3, mapping = aes(x = Distance_km, y = kin, col = Kinship_Region_Label))+
  geom_point()+
  geom_smooth(method='lm',se=T)
D

# Save the updated kinship dataframe with metadata
setwd(kinship_result_dir)
write.csv(kin_df3,file="PostPcair-KING-kinship-coeffs-with-metadata-unrel.csv", row.names = FALSE)

```

```{r Kinship - stats}

# Import kinship dataframe with metadata added
setwd(kinship_result_dir)
kin_df <- read.csv(file = "PostPcair-KING-kinship-coeffs-with-metadata-unrel.csv",header = TRUE)

# SEXES

# Calculate means and 95% C.I.'s
summary_stats <- kin_df %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Kinship_Sex_Label) %>%
  dplyr::summarize(mean_value = mean(kin, na.rm = TRUE),
            ci_lower = mean_value - 1.96 * std.error(kin, na.rm = TRUE),
            ci_upper = mean_value + 1.96 * std.error(kin, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()
summary_stats

# save results
setwd(kinship_result_dir)
write.csv(summary_stats,file = "Mean-kinship-unrel-SEXES.csv",row.names = FALSE)

# SITES

# Calculate means and 95% C.I.'s
summary_stats <- kin_df %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Kinship_Site_Label) %>%
  dplyr::summarize(mean_value = mean(kin, na.rm = TRUE),
            ci_lower = mean_value - 1.96 * std.error(kin, na.rm = TRUE),
            ci_upper = mean_value + 1.96 * std.error(kin, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()
summary_stats

# save results
setwd(kinship_result_dir)
write.csv(summary_stats,file = "Mean-kinship-unrel-SITES.csv",row.names = FALSE)

# REGIONS

# Calculate means and 95% C.I.'s
summary_stats <- kin_df %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Kinship_Region_Label) %>%
  dplyr::summarize(mean_value = mean(kin, na.rm = TRUE),
            ci_lower = mean_value - 1.96 * std.error(kin, na.rm = TRUE),
            ci_upper = mean_value + 1.96 * std.error(kin, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()
summary_stats

# save results
setwd(kinship_result_dir)
write.csv(summary_stats,file = "Mean-kinship-unrel-REGIONS.csv",row.names = FALSE)

# REGIONS SPECIFIC

# Calculate means and 95% C.I.'s
summary_stats <- kin_df %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Kinship_Region2_Label) %>%
  dplyr::summarize(mean_value = mean(kin, na.rm = TRUE),
            ci_lower = mean_value - 1.96 * std.error(kin, na.rm = TRUE),
            ci_upper = mean_value + 1.96 * std.error(kin, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()
summary_stats

# save results
setwd(kinship_result_dir)
write.csv(summary_stats,file = "Mean-kinship-unrel-REGIONS-SPECIFIC.csv",row.names = FALSE)

# SEASONS

# Calculate means and 95% C.I.'s
summary_stats <- kin_df %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Kinship_Season_Label) %>%
  dplyr::summarize(mean_value = mean(kin, na.rm = TRUE),
            ci_lower = mean_value - 1.96 * std.error(kin, na.rm = TRUE),
            ci_upper = mean_value + 1.96 * std.error(kin, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()
summary_stats

# save results
setwd(kinship_result_dir)
write.csv(summary_stats,file = "Mean-kinship-unrel-SEASONS.csv",row.names = FALSE)

# SEASONS SPECIFIC

# Calculate means and 95% C.I.'s
summary_stats <- kin_df %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Kinship_Season2_Label) %>%
  dplyr::summarize(mean_value = mean(kin, na.rm = TRUE),
            ci_lower = mean_value - 1.96 * std.error(kin, na.rm = TRUE),
            ci_upper = mean_value + 1.96 * std.error(kin, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()
summary_stats

# save results
setwd(kinship_result_dir)
write.csv(summary_stats,file = "Mean-kinship-unrel-SEASONS-SPECIFIC.csv",row.names = FALSE)

```

```{r Kinship - permutation tests & plots}

# Import kinship dataframe with metadata
setwd(kinship_result_dir)
kin_df <- read.csv(file = "PostPcair-KING-kinship-coeffs-with-metadata-unrel.csv",header = TRUE)

### FUNCTION

kin_permutation_test <- function(kinship_df, pop1, pop2, num_permutations, pop_column_name, kinship_column_name) {
  pop_column_index <- which(names(kinship_df) == pop_column_name)
  kinship_column_index <- which(names(kinship_df) == kinship_column_name)
  permuted_mean_differences <- numeric(num_permutations)
  observed_mean_difference <- mean(kinship_df[kinship_df[, pop_column_index] == pop1, kinship_column_index]) -
                              mean(kinship_df[kinship_df[, pop_column_index] == pop2, kinship_column_index])
  set.seed(123)
  for (i in 1:num_permutations) {
    shuffled_df <- kinship_df
    shuffled_df[, pop_column_index] <- sample(shuffled_df[, pop_column_index])
    permuted_mean_difference <- mean(shuffled_df[shuffled_df[, pop_column_index] == pop1, kinship_column_index]) -
                                mean(shuffled_df[shuffled_df[, pop_column_index] == pop2, kinship_column_index])
    permuted_mean_differences[i] <- permuted_mean_difference
  }
  p_value <- 2 * min(
    mean(permuted_mean_differences >= observed_mean_difference),
    mean(permuted_mean_differences <= observed_mean_difference)
  )
  permuted_data <- data.frame(permuted_mean_difference = permuted_mean_differences)
  return(list(
    p_value = p_value,
    permuted_data = permuted_data,
    observed_mean_difference = observed_mean_difference
  ))
}



### TEST - SEXES

# Remove between-sex comparisons
kin_df2 <- kin_df %>% filter(Kinship_Sex_Label %in% c("Between-males","Between-females"))

# Permutation test (uses function from earlier chunk)
kin_perm_result <- kin_permutation_test(kinship_df = kin_df2, pop1 = "Between-males", pop2 = "Between-females", num_permutations = 1000, pop_column_name = "Kinship_Sex_Label", kinship_column_name = "kin")

# Save the results
setwd(kinship_result_dir)
sink(file = "Kinship-permutation-result-unrel-SEX.txt")
cat("Observed Mean Difference:", kin_perm_result$observed_mean_difference, "\n")
cat("Two-sided p-value:", kin_perm_result$p_value, "\n")
sink()
write.csv(kin_perm_result$permuted_data,file = "Kinship-permutation-bootstraps-unrel-SEX.csv",row.names = FALSE)



### TEST - SITES

# Permutation test (uses function from earlier chunk)
kin_perm_result <- kin_permutation_test(kinship_df = kin_df, pop1 = "Between-sites", pop2 = "Within-sites", num_permutations = 1000, pop_column_name = "Kinship_Site_Label", kinship_column_name = "kin")

# Save the results
setwd(kinship_result_dir)
sink(file = "Kinship-permutation-result-unrel-SITES.txt")
cat("Observed Mean Difference:", kin_perm_result$observed_mean_difference, "\n")
cat("Two-sided p-value:", kin_perm_result$p_value, "\n")
sink()
write.csv(kin_perm_result$permuted_data,file = "Kinship-permutation-bootstraps-unrel-SITES.csv",row.names = FALSE)



### TEST - REGIONS

# Permutation test (uses function from earlier chunk)
kin_perm_result <- kin_permutation_test(kinship_df = kin_df, pop1 = "Between-regions", pop2 = "Within-regions", num_permutations = 1000, pop_column_name = "Kinship_Region_Label", kinship_column_name = "kin")

# Save the results
setwd(kinship_result_dir)
sink(file = "Kinship-permutation-result-unrel-REGIONS.txt")
cat("Observed Mean Difference:", kin_perm_result$observed_mean_difference, "\n")
cat("Two-sided p-value:", kin_perm_result$p_value, "\n")
sink()
write.csv(kin_perm_result$permuted_data,file = "Kinship-permutation-bootstraps-unrel-REGIONS.csv",row.names = FALSE)



### TEST - REGIONS

pop1 <- "North"
pop2 <- "Central"
pop3 <- "South"
region_list <- list(pop1,pop2,pop3)

# Remove between-region comparisons
kin_df2 <- kin_df %>% filter(Kinship_Region2_Label %in% region_list)

# Convert 'pop' to factor with custom order for plotting
kin_df2$Kinship_Region2_Label <- factor(kin_df2$Kinship_Region2_Label, levels = region_list)

# Permutation tests
pop_comp1 <- paste(pop1,pop2,sep="-") # Name to save results and plots
kin_perm_result_1 <- kin_permutation_test(kinship_df = kin_df2, pop1 = pop1, pop2 = pop2, num_permutations = 1000, pop_column_name = "Kinship_Region2_Label", kinship_column_name = "kin")
pop_comp2 <- paste(pop1,pop3,sep="-") # Name to save results and plots
kin_perm_result_2 <- kin_permutation_test(kinship_df = kin_df2, pop1 = pop1, pop2 = pop3, num_permutations = 1000, pop_column_name = "Kinship_Region2_Label", kinship_column_name = "kin")
pop_comp3 <- paste(pop2,pop3,sep="-") # Name to save results and plots
kin_perm_result_3 <- kin_permutation_test(kinship_df = kin_df2, pop1 = pop2, pop2 = pop3, num_permutations = 1000, pop_column_name = "Kinship_Region2_Label", kinship_column_name = "kin")

# Save the results
setwd(kinship_result_dir)
sink(file = "Kinship-permutation-result-unrel-REGIONS-specific.txt")
cat(print(pop_comp1), "\n")
cat("Observed Mean Difference:", kin_perm_result_1$observed_mean_difference, "\n")
cat("Two-sided p-value:", kin_perm_result_1$p_value, "\n")
cat(print(pop_comp2), "\n")
cat("Observed Mean Difference:", kin_perm_result_2$observed_mean_difference, "\n")
cat("Two-sided p-value:", kin_perm_result_2$p_value, "\n")
cat(print(pop_comp3), "\n")
cat("Observed Mean Difference:", kin_perm_result_3$observed_mean_difference, "\n")
cat("Two-sided p-value:", kin_perm_result_3$p_value, "\n")
sink()
write.csv(kin_perm_result_1$permuted_data,file = paste("Kinship-permutation-bootstraps-unrel-REGIONS-",pop_comp1,".csv",sep=""),row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(kin_perm_result_2$permuted_data,file = paste("Kinship-permutation-bootstraps-unrel-REGIONS-",pop_comp2,".csv",sep=""),row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(kin_perm_result_3$permuted_data,file = paste("Kinship-permutation-bootstraps-unrel-REGIONS-",pop_comp3,".csv",sep=""),row.names = FALSE)


### TESTS FOR MALES AND FEMALES SEPARATELY

kin_df2 <- kin_df %>% filter(Kinship_Sex_Label %in% c("Between-males","Between-females"))



### TEST - MALES - SITES

# Males only
kin_df3 <- kin_df2 %>% filter(Kinship_Sex_Label == "Between-males")

# Permutation tests
kin_perm_result <- kin_permutation_test(kinship_df = kin_df3, pop1 = "Between-sites", pop2 = "Within-sites", num_permutations = 1000, pop_column_name = "Kinship_Site_Label", kinship_column_name = "kin")

# Save the results
setwd(kinship_result_dir)
sink(file = "Kinship-permutation-result-unrel-SITES-MALES.txt")
cat("Observed Mean Difference:", kin_perm_result$observed_mean_difference, "\n")
cat("Two-sided p-value:", kin_perm_result$p_value, "\n")
sink()
write.csv(kin_perm_result$permuted_data,file = "Kinship-permutation-bootstraps-unrel-SITES-MALES.csv",row.names = FALSE)



### TEST - FEMALES - SITES

# Females only
kin_df3 <- kin_df2 %>% filter(Kinship_Sex_Label == "Between-females")

# Permutation tests
kin_perm_result <- kin_permutation_test(kinship_df = kin_df3, pop1 = "Between-sites", pop2 = "Within-sites", num_permutations = 1000, pop_column_name = "Kinship_Site_Label", kinship_column_name = "kin")

# Save the results
setwd(kinship_result_dir)
sink(file = "Kinship-permutation-result-unrel-SITES-FEMALES.txt")
cat("Observed Mean Difference:", kin_perm_result$observed_mean_difference, "\n")
cat("Two-sided p-value:", kin_perm_result$p_value, "\n")
sink()
write.csv(kin_perm_result$permuted_data,file = "Kinship-permutation-bootstraps-unrel-SITES-FEMALES.csv",row.names = FALSE)



### TEST - MALES - REGIONS

# Males only
kin_df3 <- kin_df2 %>% filter(Kinship_Sex_Label == "Between-males")

# Permutation tests
kin_perm_result <- kin_permutation_test(kinship_df = kin_df3, pop1 = "Between-regions", pop2 = "Within-regions", num_permutations = 1000, pop_column_name = "Kinship_Region_Label", kinship_column_name = "kin")

# Save the results
setwd(kinship_result_dir)
sink(file = "Kinship-permutation-result-unrel-REGIONS-MALES.txt")
cat("Observed Mean Difference:", kin_perm_result$observed_mean_difference, "\n")
cat("Two-sided p-value:", kin_perm_result$p_value, "\n")
sink()
write.csv(kin_perm_result$permuted_data,file = "Kinship-permutation-bootstraps-unrel-REGIONS-MALES.csv",row.names = FALSE)



### TEST - FEMALES - REGIONS

# Females only
kin_df3 <- kin_df2 %>% filter(Kinship_Sex_Label == "Between-females")

# Permutation tests
kin_perm_result <- kin_permutation_test(kinship_df = kin_df3, pop1 = "Between-regions", pop2 = "Within-regions", num_permutations = 1000, pop_column_name = "Kinship_Region_Label", kinship_column_name = "kin")

# Save the results
setwd(kinship_result_dir)
sink(file = "Kinship-permutation-result-unrel-REGIONS-FEMALES.txt")
cat("Observed Mean Difference:", kin_perm_result$observed_mean_difference, "\n")
cat("Two-sided p-value:", kin_perm_result$p_value, "\n")
sink()
write.csv(kin_perm_result$permuted_data,file = "Kinship-permutation-bootstraps-unrel-REGIONS-FEMALES.csv",row.names = FALSE)



### TEST - MALES - SPECIFIC REGIONS

# Remove between-region comparisons
kin_df2 <- kin_df %>% filter(Kinship_Region2_Label %in% region_list)

# Males only
kin_df3 <- kin_df2 %>% filter(Kinship_Sex_Label == "Between-males")

# Permutation tests
pop_comp1 <- paste(pop1,pop2,sep="-") # Name to save results and plots
kin_perm_result_1 <- kin_permutation_test(kinship_df = kin_df3, pop1 = pop1, pop2 = pop2, num_permutations = 1000, pop_column_name = "Kinship_Region2_Label", kinship_column_name = "kin")
pop_comp2 <- paste(pop1,pop3,sep="-") # Name to save results and plots
kin_perm_result_2 <- kin_permutation_test(kinship_df = kin_df3, pop1 = pop1, pop2 = pop3, num_permutations = 1000, pop_column_name = "Kinship_Region2_Label", kinship_column_name = "kin")
pop_comp3 <- paste(pop2,pop3,sep="-") # Name to save results and plots
kin_perm_result_3 <- kin_permutation_test(kinship_df = kin_df3, pop1 = pop2, pop2 = pop3, num_permutations = 1000, pop_column_name = "Kinship_Region2_Label", kinship_column_name = "kin")

# Save the results
setwd(kinship_result_dir)
sink(file = "Kinship-permutation-result-unrel-REGIONS-specific-MALES.txt")
cat(print(pop_comp1), "\n")
cat("Observed Mean Difference:", kin_perm_result_1$observed_mean_difference, "\n")
cat("Two-sided p-value:", kin_perm_result_1$p_value, "\n")
cat(print(pop_comp2), "\n")
cat("Observed Mean Difference:", kin_perm_result_2$observed_mean_difference, "\n")
cat("Two-sided p-value:", kin_perm_result_2$p_value, "\n")
cat(print(pop_comp3), "\n")
cat("Observed Mean Difference:", kin_perm_result_3$observed_mean_difference, "\n")
cat("Two-sided p-value:", kin_perm_result_3$p_value, "\n")
sink()
write.csv(kin_perm_result_1$permuted_data,file = paste("Kinship-permutation-bootstraps-unrel-REGIONS-MALES-",pop_comp1,".csv",sep=""),row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(kin_perm_result_2$permuted_data,file = paste("Kinship-permutation-bootstraps-unrel-REGIONS-MALES-",pop_comp2,".csv",sep=""),row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(kin_perm_result_3$permuted_data,file = paste("Kinship-permutation-bootstraps-unrel-REGIONS-MALES-",pop_comp3,".csv",sep=""),row.names = FALSE)



### TESTS - FEMALES - SPECIFIC REGIONS

# Females only
kin_df3 <- kin_df2 %>% filter(Kinship_Sex_Label == "Between-females")

# Permutation tests
pop_comp1 <- paste(pop1,pop2,sep="-") # Name to save results and plots
kin_perm_result_1 <- kin_permutation_test(kinship_df = kin_df3, pop1 = pop1, pop2 = pop2, num_permutations = 1000, pop_column_name = "Kinship_Region2_Label", kinship_column_name = "kin")
pop_comp2 <- paste(pop1,pop3,sep="-") # Name to save results and plots
kin_perm_result_2 <- kin_permutation_test(kinship_df = kin_df3, pop1 = pop1, pop2 = pop3, num_permutations = 1000, pop_column_name = "Kinship_Region2_Label", kinship_column_name = "kin")
pop_comp3 <- paste(pop2,pop3,sep="-") # Name to save results and plots
kin_perm_result_3 <- kin_permutation_test(kinship_df = kin_df3, pop1 = pop2, pop2 = pop3, num_permutations = 1000, pop_column_name = "Kinship_Region2_Label", kinship_column_name = "kin")

# Save the results
setwd(kinship_result_dir)
sink(file = "Kinship-permutation-result-unrel-REGIONS-specific-FEMALES.txt")
cat(print(pop_comp1), "\n")
cat("Observed Mean Difference:", kin_perm_result_1$observed_mean_difference, "\n")
cat("Two-sided p-value:", kin_perm_result_1$p_value, "\n")
cat(print(pop_comp2), "\n")
cat("Observed Mean Difference:", kin_perm_result_2$observed_mean_difference, "\n")
cat("Two-sided p-value:", kin_perm_result_2$p_value, "\n")
cat(print(pop_comp3), "\n")
cat("Observed Mean Difference:", kin_perm_result_3$observed_mean_difference, "\n")
cat("Two-sided p-value:", kin_perm_result_3$p_value, "\n")
sink()
write.csv(kin_perm_result_1$permuted_data,file = paste("Kinship-permutation-bootstraps-unrel-REGIONS-FEMALES-",pop_comp1,".csv",sep=""),row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(kin_perm_result_2$permuted_data,file = paste("Kinship-permutation-bootstraps-unrel-REGIONS-FEMALES-",pop_comp2,".csv",sep=""),row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(kin_perm_result_3$permuted_data,file = paste("Kinship-permutation-bootstraps-unrel-REGIONS-FEMALES-",pop_comp3,".csv",sep=""),row.names = FALSE)





### PLOTS

# SEX

kin_df2 <- kin_df %>% filter(Kinship_Sex_Label %in% c("Between-males","Between-females"))

# Kinship by sex - Mean and 95% CI plot
A <- ggplot(kin_df2, aes(x = Kinship_Sex_Label, y = kin, color = Kinship_Sex_Label)) +
  stat_summary(fun.data = "mean_cl_normal", geom = "errorbar", width = 0.2) + # Add a mean layer with 95% confidence intervals
  stat_summary(fun.y = "mean", geom = "point", size = 3, shape = 20, fill = "white") + # Add a point layer for the mean value
  labs(x = "Sex", y = "Mean kinship") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#FFCD00", "#00843D"), name = "") +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("F","M"))
A

# SITES

# Kinship by site - Mean and 95% CI plot
B <- ggplot(kin_df, aes(x = Kinship_Site_Label, y = kin, color = Kinship_Site_Label)) +
  stat_summary(fun.data = "mean_cl_normal", geom = "errorbar", width = 0.2) + # Add a mean layer with 95% confidence intervals
  stat_summary(fun.y = "mean", geom = "point", size = 3, shape = 20, fill = "white") + # Add a point layer for the mean value
  labs(x = "Localities", y = "Mean kinship") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("red", "blue"), name = "") +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("Between","Within"))
B

# REGIONS

# Kinship by regions - Mean and 95% CI plot
C <- ggplot(kin_df, aes(x = Kinship_Region_Label, y = kin, color = Kinship_Region_Label)) +
  stat_summary(fun.data = "mean_cl_normal", geom = "errorbar", width = 0.2) + # Add a mean layer with 95% confidence intervals
  stat_summary(fun.y = "mean", geom = "point", size = 3, shape = 20, fill = "white") + # Add a point layer for the mean value
  labs(x = "Regions", y = "Mean kinship") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("red", "blue"), name = "") +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("Between","Within"))
C

# REGIONS SPECIFIC

# Remove between-region comparisons
kin_df2 <- kin_df %>% filter(Kinship_Region2_Label %in% region_list)

# Convert 'pop' to factor with custom order for plotting
kin_df2$Kinship_Region2_Label <- factor(kin_df2$Kinship_Region2_Label, levels = region_list)

# Basic plot - Mean and 95% CI plot
D <- ggplot(kin_df2, aes(x = Kinship_Region2_Label, y = kin, color = Kinship_Region2_Label)) +
  stat_summary(fun.data = "mean_cl_normal", geom = "errorbar", width = 0.2) + # Add a mean layer with 95% confidence intervals
  stat_summary(fun.y = "mean", geom = "point", size = 3, shape = 20, fill = "white") + # Add a point layer for the mean value
  labs(x = "Regions", y = "Mean kinship") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#2176ae","#ce4257","#1A936F"), name = "") +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = region_list)
D


# SEASONS PLOT

G <- ggplot(kin_df2, aes(x = Kinship_Season2_Label, y = kin, color = Kinship_Season2_Label)) +
  stat_summary(fun.data = "mean_cl_normal", geom = "errorbar", width = 0.2) + # Add a mean layer with 95% confidence intervals
  stat_summary(fun.y = "mean", geom = "point", size = 3, shape = 20, fill = "white") + # Add a point layer for the mean value
  labs(x = "Season collected", y = "Mean kinship") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("red", "blue","green","orange"), name = "Group")+
  facet_grid(~ Kinship_Region2_Label)+
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("Between","Dry","Early Wet","Wet"))
G

# Save plots

plot_margin <- theme(plot.margin=margin(t = 10, r = 10, b = 5, l = 10, unit = "pt"))
plot_theme <- theme(panel.grid.major = element_blank(), panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), panel.background = element_rect(fill = NA), plot.background = element_rect(fill=NA), panel.border = element_rect(fill=NA),legend.position = "none") 
text_size <- theme(axis.title.x = element_text(size = 10), axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8), axis.title.y = element_text(size = 10), axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),plot.title=element_text(size=11,face="bold",hjust=0),legend.text = element_text(size=6))

# All samples
A2 <- A + plot_margin + plot_theme + text_size
B2 <- B + plot_margin + plot_theme + text_size
C2 <- C + plot_margin + plot_theme + text_size
D2 <- D + plot_margin + plot_theme + text_size
G2 <- G + plot_margin + plot_theme + text_size

## TIFF and sized for Science Journal
setwd(paste0(kinship_result_dir,"/_PLOTS"))
tiff("Kinship-multi-plots-unrel-AllSamples.tif",width = 12, height = 10, unit='cm', res=300)
ggarrange(A2,B2,C2,D2,
          labels = c("A","B","C","D"),
          ncol = 2, nrow = 2)
dev.off()


# Seasons plot
setwd(paste0(kinship_result_dir,"/_PLOTS"))
tiff("Kinship-multi-plots-unrel-AllSamples-seasons2.tif",width = 17.5, height = 6, unit='cm', res=300)
ggarrange(G2,
          labels = c(""),
          ncol = 1, nrow = 1)
dev.off()

```
